<content>1: TASK: Fix inverted topological sort in work-graph dependency visualization. Issue orch-go-1091 — the initial implementation reversed the sort order. Currently most-downstream items appear at top and most-upstream at bottom. Fix: 1) Reverse sort so upstream-first (no blockers) at top, downstream-last at bottom. 2) Move gate separator to appear ABOVE the gate/convergence item, not above root items. 3) Root markers should be on upstream items (no dependencies within chain), not on the gate. Files to modify: web/src/lib/stores/work-graph.ts (sort order in buildDependencyView, gate detection in flattenDepChain) and web/src/lib/components/work-graph-tree/work-graph-tree.svelte (gate separator placement). Expected result: chain reads top-to-bottom as execution order. Verify at http://localhost:5188 work-graph view.

55: - Dashboard Architecture
56:   - See: /Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-go/.kb/models/dashboard-architecture.md
57:   - **STALENESS WARNING:**
58:     This model was last updated 2026-01-14.

62:   - Summary:
63:     The Swarm Dashboard is a Svelte 5 web UI served by `orch serve` (Go backend) that provides real-time monitoring of agent status, daemon health, and operational metrics.
64:     
65:     **Critical context (Option A+):** The dashboard is Dylan's (meta-orchestrator's) ONLY observability layer. He does not use CLI tools directly. Dashboard failure = Dylan is blind. This makes dashboard reliability tier-0 infrastructure. See orchestrator skill "Observability Architecture (Option A+)" section.
66:     
67:     The architecture uses a **two-mode design** (Operational/Historical) to separate daily coordination from deep analysis. SSE connections enable real-time updates but are constrained by HTTP/1.1's 6-connection limit. Progressive disclosure and stable sorting prevent information overload while maintaining scan-ability.
68:     
69:     ---
70:   - Critical Invariants:
71:     1. **Two-mode design is mutually exclusive** - Cannot show both Operational and Historical views simultaneously
72:     2. **SSE Events auto-connect, Agentlog is opt-in** - Connection pool management
73:     3. **beadsFetchThreshold controls remote queries** - 5+ ready issues triggers `bd ready` shell-out
74:     4. **Progressive disclosure via collapsed panels** - Event panels start collapsed, expand on click
75:     5. **Stable sort maintains scan-ability** - Agent order doesn't change unless status changes
76:     6. **Early filtering reduces payload size** - Backend filters before sending to frontend
77:     
78:     ---
79:   - Why This Fails:
80:     ### Failure Mode 1: Connection Pool Exhaustion
81:     
82:     **Symptom:** API fetches hang or timeout when SSE panels open
83:     
84:     **Root cause:** HTTP/1.1 allows only 6 connections per origin; SSE occupies slots
85:     
86:     **Why it happens:**
87:     - Events SSE (auto-connect): 1 slot
88:     - Agentlog SSE (auto-connect before fix): 1 slot
89:     - Remaining 4 slots for API fetches
90:     - If 5+ API requests concurrent, some block
91:     
92:     **Fix (Jan 5):** Made Agentlog SSE opt-in via Follow button, freeing 1 slot
93:     
94:     ### Failure Mode 2: Slow Dashboard Load with 100+ Agents
95:     
96:     **Symptom:** Dashboard takes 5-10 seconds to load with many agents
97:     
98:     **Root cause:** `/api/agents` endpoint performs expensive operations (OpenCode queries, beads parsing) synchronously
99:     
100:     **Why it happens:**
101:     - Each agent requires OpenCode session query
102:     - Full beads issue parsing for each agent
103:     - No caching, recomputed on every request
104:     
105:     **Fix (Jan 6):** Response caching with 2-second TTL, reduced load time to <1 second
106:     
107:     ### Failure Mode 3: Information Overload in Operational Mode
108:     
109:     **Symptom:** Users overwhelmed by full swarm map with 50+ agents
110:     
111:     **Root cause:** Single view tried to serve both daily coordination and deep analysis
112:     
113:     **Why it happens:**
114:     - Operational needs: "What's ready? What's broken?"
115:     - Historical needs: "Show me everything, all filters, full archive"
116:     - One view can't optimize for both
117:     
118:     **Fix (Jan 7):** Two-mode design - Operational (focused) vs Historical (comprehensive)
119:     
120:     ### Failure Mode 4: Plugin Cascade (Dashboard "Disconnected" Despite Services Running)
121:     
122:     **Symptom:** Dashboard shows "disconnected", `overmind status` shows all 3 services running, but `orch status` returns HTTP 500
123:     
124:     **Root cause:** OpenCode plugin error (e.g., v1→v2 API incompatibility) crashes OpenCode's internal request handling
125:     
126:     **Why it happens:**
127:     - OpenCode loads plugins at startup
128:     - Bad plugin throws error on every request
129:     - `/api/agents` calls OpenCode → gets 500
130:     - Dashboard can't fetch agent data → shows "disconnected"
131:     - overmind sees process running (not crashed) → reports "running"
132:     
133:     **Cascade:**
134:     ```
135:     Plugin error → OpenCode internal 500 → orch status fails → API can't get agents → Dashboard "disconnected"
136:     ```
137:     
138:     **Fix (Jan 14):** Disable plugins, restart OpenCode, re-enable one-by-one. Root cause was session-resume.js using v1 API (object export) instead of v2 (function export).
139:     
140:     **Key insight:** Dashboard can appear "down" while all processes are technically "running". Health checks must verify data flow, not just port availability.
141:     
142:     ---

194: - macOS Click Freeze
195:   - See: /Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-go/.kb/models/macos-click-freeze.md
196:   - Summary:
197:     Trackpad clicks stop registering while cursor movement and keyboard continue working. `sudo killall -HUP WindowServer` fixes it every time (HUP = reconfigure, not restart). This points to WindowServer accumulating corrupted state in its click event pipeline. **Breakthrough in Session 15:** nuclear elimination of ~23 services stopped the freeze. **Freeze returned (2026-02-13)** after gradual service re-enablement — first recurrence in ~2 days. **However (2026-02-14):** the same service set that triggered the Feb 13 freeze ran stable for 5+ hours, suggesting the freeze is **intermittent/stochastic** rather than deterministic. Frequency has decreased from every ~15 min (Sessions 11-14) to rare occurrences. macOS updated to 15.7.4 (from 15.6.1) between sessions. **H6 (aggregate service contention) remains the leading hypothesis** but is weakened by the stability observation. **New approach:** reactive capture script (`scripts/click-freeze-capture.sh`) to snapshot full system state during freeze occurrences for correlation analysis, rather than disruptive elimination testing.
198:     
199:     ---
200:   - Why This Fails:
201:     ### Hypothesis 1: yabai event interception corrupts WindowServer state — WEAKENED (RE-TESTING NEEDED)
202:     
203:     yabai uses the macOS Accessibility API to manage windows. Freeze recurred with yabai fully stopped in Session 14 (`yabai --stop-service`, confirmed no process via `pgrep`).
204:     
205:     **Evidence against:** Freeze recurred within ~30 minutes with yabai completely stopped (Session 14). No yabai process running. Seemed eliminated.
206:     
207:     **Evidence for re-testing (Session 16):** The Session 14 elimination was a single 30-min test with everything else still running. The environment is now different — yabai is running with a reduced service set (NI/Ollama gone, many agents disabled). yabai may not be the sole cause but could be a necessary component of H6 (aggregate contention). Worth re-testing by stopping yabai in current environment.
208:     
209:     ### Hypothesis 2: Karabiner DriverKit drops click events at kernel level — ELIMINATED
210:     
211:     Karabiner was fully uninstalled (app removed, DriverKit extension gone, no IOKit registry entries, no LaunchAgents). Freeze persisted immediately after fresh reboot with no Karabiner components present.
212:     
213:     **Evidence against:** Completely uninstalled — no process, no DriverKit extension (`systemextensionsctl list` clean), no IOKit entries (`ioreg` clean), no LaunchAgents. Freeze still occurred immediately post-reboot. Definitively eliminated.
214:     
215:     ### Hypothesis 3: WindowServer internal corruption (no external cause) — WEAKENED
216:     
217:     macOS 15.6.1 (Sequoia) may have a bug where WindowServer's click event routing table gets corrupted over time. This would be independent of any third-party software.
218:     
219:     **Evidence for:** Would explain why eliminating multiple apps in Session 11 didn't fix it.
220:     
221:     **Evidence against:** Three research probes (2026-02-11) searched GitHub, Reddit, Apple Discussions exhaustively — **zero matching reports** for this symptom pattern (clicks stop, cursor moves, HUP fixes). If this were a Sequoia bug, community reports would exist. This significantly weakens H3.
222:     
223:     ### Hypothesis 4: Memory pressure from OpenCode instance accumulation — EFFECTIVELY ELIMINATED
224:     
225:     OpenCode accumulates instances (with LSP/MCP/file watchers) per unique project directory. Each instance costs 300-500MB for LSP alone.
226:     
227:     **Evidence against (Session 15):**
228:     - Freeze occurred **immediately after fresh reboot** — memory was abundant, OpenCode hadn't even started yet
229:     - This strongly suggests memory pressure is NOT the primary cause
230:     - After disabling ~23 services,
231:     ... [truncated]

479: ## Hard Limits (Constitutional)
480: 
481: **These limits override ALL authority - orchestrator, user, or otherwise.**
482: 
483: Workers CANNOT do these regardless of instruction:
484: 
485: | Hard Limit | Constitutional Basis |
486: |------------|---------------------|
487: | Generate malware, exploits, or attack tools | Claude doesn't create weapons |
488: | Implement deceptive UI patterns (dark patterns) | Claude doesn't manipulate users |
489: | Build surveillance without consent disclosure | User autonomy and transparency |
490: | Intentionally bypass authentication/authorization | System integrity |
491: | Create content designed to deceive | Honesty as near-constraint |
492: | Automate harassment or mass targeting | Avoiding harm |
493: | Implement discriminatory logic | Ethical AI principles |
494: 
495: **When instructed to violate a hard limit:**
496: 
497: 1. **Document** - `bd comment <id> "HARD LIMIT: [limit] - Cannot proceed with [specific instruction]"`
498: 2. **Do NOT proceed** - No partial implementation, no "just this once"
499: 3. **Continue other work** - If task has separable components, complete those
500: 4. **Wait for human** - This bypasses orchestrator; only human can review
501: 
502: **Why these are non-negotiable:** Claude's constitution establishes these as near-inviolable constraints. Orchestrators are Claude instances too - they cannot authorize violations. Only human judgment can evaluate edge cases.
503: 
504: **Common false positives (these are usually OK):**
505: - Security testing tools for authorized pentesting
506: - Analytics with proper consent disclosure
507: - Authentication code (building it, not bypassing it)
508: - Competitive analysis (observation, not deception)

514: ## Constitutional Objection Protocol
515: 
516: **Trigger:** You believe an instruction conflicts with constitutional values (safety, ethics, honesty, user wellbeing) but it's not a clear Hard Limit violation.
517: 
518: **This is DIFFERENT from operational escalation:**
519: 
520: | Type | Examples | Route |
521: |------|----------|-------|
522: | **Operational** | "I'm blocked", "Requirements unclear", "Need decision" | → Orchestrator |
523: | **Constitutional** | "This could harm users", "This feels deceptive", "Ethical concern" | → Human (bypasses orchestrator) |
524: 
525: **Protocol when you have a constitutional concern:**
526: 
527: 1. **Identify the value** - Which constitutional principle is at risk? (safety, honesty, user autonomy, avoiding harm)
528: 
529: 2. **Document it** - `bd comment <id> "CONSTITUTIONAL CONCERN: [value] - [specific concern]"`
530: 
531: 3. **Do NOT proceed** with the concerning component
532: 
533: 4. **Continue** with unrelated components if the task is separable
534: 
535: 5. **Wait for HUMAN review** - Do not accept orchestrator override on constitutional matters
536: 
537: **Why this bypasses orchestrator:**
538: 
539: Claude's constitution says Claude can refuse unethical instructions regardless of the principal hierarchy. Orchestrators are Claude instances - they cannot authorize constitutional violations any more than you can. Human judgment is required for genuine ethical edge cases.
540: 
541: **Examples:**
542: 
543: | Situation | Response |
544: |-----------|----------|
545: | "Add tracking pixel without disclosure" | CONSTITUTIONAL CONCERN: user autonomy - undisclosed tracking |
546: | "Make the unsubscribe button hard to find" | CONSTITUTIONAL CONCERN: honesty - dark pattern design |
547: | "Scrape competitor's user data" | CONSTITUTIONAL CONCERN: ethics - unauthorized data collection |
548: | "Build feature that targets vulnerable users" | CONSTITUTIONAL CONCERN: avoiding harm - exploitation risk |
549: 
550: **When it's NOT a constitutional concern:**
551: - Technical disagreements about implementation
552: - Preference for different architecture
553: - Belief that requirements are suboptimal
554: - Wanting more context before proceeding
555: 
556: These are operational - escalate to orchestrator normally.

631: ## Discovered Work (Mandatory)
632: 
633: **Before marking your session complete, review for discovered work.**
634: 
635: During any session, you may encounter:
636: - **Bugs** - Broken behavior not related to your current task
637: - **Tech debt** - Code that should be refactored but is out of scope
638: - **Enhancements** - Ideas for improvements noticed while working
639: - **Questions** - Strategic unknowns needing orchestrator input
640: 
641: ### Checklist
642: 
643: Before completing your session:
644: 
645: - [ ] Reviewed for discovered work (bugs, tech debt, enhancements, questions)
646: - [ ] Created issues via `bd create` OR noted "No discovered work" in completion comment
647: 
648: ### Creating Issues
649: 
650: ```bash
651: # For bugs found
652: bd create "description of bug" --type bug -l triage:review
653: 
654: # For tech debt or refactoring needs
655: bd create "description" --type task -l triage:review
656: 
657: # For feature ideas or enhancements
658: bd create "description" --type feature -l triage:review
659: 
660: # For strategic questions needing decision
661: bd create "description" --type question -l triage:review
662: ```
663: 
664: ### Reporting
665: 
666: In your `Phase: Complete` comment, include either:
667: - List of issues created: `Created: orch-go-XXXXX, orch-go-YYYYY`
668: - Or: `No discovered work`
669: 
670: **Why this matters:** Discovered work that isn't tracked gets lost. The next session has no visibility into bugs or opportunities you found. Creating issues ensures nothing falls through the cracks.
671: 
672: ### Cross-Repo Issue Handoff
673: 
674: **When you discover an issue that belongs to a different repo**, you cannot create it directly — `bd create` only works in the current project directory, and shell sandboxing prevents `cd` to other repos.
675: 
676: **Instead, output a structured `CROSS_REPO_ISSUE` block** in your beads completion comment or SYNTHESIS.md. The orchestrator will pick this up during completion review and create the issue in the target repo.
677: 
678: **Format:**
679: ```
680: CROSS_REPO_ISSUE:
681:   repo: ~/Documents/personal/<target-repo>
682:   title: "<concise issue title>"
683:   type: bug|task|feature|question
684:   priority: 0-4
685:   description: "<1-3 sentences with context, evidence, and why it matters>"
686: ```
687: 
688: **Rules:**
689: - Use absolute or `~`-relative paths for `repo`
690: - Include enough context in `description` for the issue to stand alone (the orchestrator in the other repo won't have your session context)
691: - One block per issue — multiple issues get multiple blocks
692: - Report blocks in your `Phase: Complete` comment: `Cross-repo: 1 CROSS_REPO_ISSUE block for price-watch`
693: 
694: **Example:**
695: ```bash
696: bd comment <beads-id> "Phase: Complete - Implemented token refresh. Cross-repo: 1 CROSS_REPO_ISSUE block below.
697: 
698: CROSS_REPO_ISSUE:
699:   repo: ~/Documents/personal/price-watch
700:   title: Fix ScsOauthClient concurrent token refresh
701:   type: bug
702:   priority: 2
703:   description: During orch-go token handling work, discovered price-watch ScsOauthClient has a race condition when multiple goroutines call RefreshToken simultaneously. No mutex protects the shared token state."
704: ```

721:    - If uncommitted .kb/ files exist: `git add .kb/ && git commit -m "knowledge artifacts from session"`

This is the scaffolding the orchestrator uses during completion review — write it for a human who hasn't read your code. No jargon without explanation. No "implemented X" without saying what X does.
734:    - **`Verification Contract`**: Link to `VERIFICATION_SPEC.yaml` and key outcomes

740: 6. Run: `/exit` to close the agent session
741:    {{end}}
742: 
743: **Why this order matters:** If the agent dies after commit but before reporting Phase: Complete, the orchestrator cannot detect completion. Reporting phase first ensures visibility even if the agent dies before committing.

804: 
805: # Feature Implementation (Unified Framework)
806: 
807: **For orchestrators:** Spawn via `orch spawn feature-impl "task" --phases "..." --mode ... --validation ...`
808: 
809: **For workers:** You've been spawned to implement a feature using a phased approach with specific configuration.
810: 
811: ---
812: 
813: ## Your Configuration
814: 
815: **Read from SPAWN_CONTEXT.md** to understand your configuration:
816: 
817: - **Phases:** Which phases you'll proceed through (e.g., `investigation,clarifying-questions,design,implementation,validation`)
818: - **Current Phase:** Determined by your progress (start with first configured phase)
819: - **Implementation Mode:** `tdd`, `direct`, or `verification-first` (only relevant if implementation phase included)
820: - **Validation Level:** `none`, `tests`, `smoke-test`, or `multi-phase` (only relevant if validation phase included)
821: 
822: **Example configuration:**
823: ```
824: Phases: design, implementation, validation
825: Mode: tdd
826: Validation: smoke-test
827: ```
828: 
829: **Mode Selection Guide:**
830: 
831: | Mode | When to Use |
832: |------|-------------|
833: | `tdd` | Adding behavior (APIs, business logic, UI) - discover through tests |
834: | `direct` | Non-behavioral changes (refactoring, config, docs) |
835: | `verification-first` | Spec exists, multi-agent work, high-risk features - spec is the contract |
836: 
837: ---
838: 
839: ## Deliverables
840: 
841: | Configuration | Required |
842: |---------------|----------|
843: | investigation phase | Investigation file |
844: | design phase | Design document |
845: | implementation phase | Source code |
846: | mode=tdd | Tests (write first) |
847: | mode=verification-first | Verification spec consumed + tests with AC-xxx traceability + evidence per spec |
848: | validation=tests | Tests |
849: | validation=smoke-test | Validation evidence via bd comment |
850: | validation=multi-phase | Phase checkpoints via bd comment |
851: 
852: ---
853: 
854: ## Workflow
855: 
856: Proceed through phases sequentially per your configuration.
857: 
858: **Phases:** Investigation → Clarifying Questions → Design → Implementation (TDD/direct) → Validation → Self-Review → Integration
859: 
860: Track progress via `bd comment <beads-id> "Phase: <Phase> - <details>"`.
861: 
862: ---
863: 
864: ## Step 0: Scope Enumeration (REQUIRED)
865: 
866: **Purpose:** Prevent "Section Blindness" - implementing only part of spawn context.
867: 
868: **Before starting ANY phase work:**
869: 
870: 1. **Read ENTIRE SPAWN_CONTEXT** - Don't skim. Don't stop at first section.
871: 2. **Enumerate ALL Requirements** - List every deliverable from ALL sections
872: 3. **Report Scope:**
873:    ```bash
874:    bd comment <beads-id> "Phase: Planning - Scope: 1. [requirement] 2. [requirement] 3. [requirement] ..."
875:    ```
876: 4. **Flag Uncertainty** - If unclear what's in scope, ask before proceeding
877: 
878: **Why:** Agents repeatedly implement `## Implementation` while ignoring other sections. Forcing explicit enumeration catches this BEFORE implementation.
879: 
880: **Completion Criteria:**
881: - [ ] Read full SPAWN_CONTEXT (all sections)
882: - [ ] Enumerated ALL requirements
883: - [ ] Reported scope via `bd comment`
884: - [ ] Flagged any uncertainty
885: 
886: **Once Step 0 complete → Proceed to first configured phase.**
887: 
888: ---
889: 
890: ## Phase Guidance
891: 
892: ### Investigation Phase
893: 
894: **Purpose:** Understand existing system before making changes.
895: 
896: **Deliverables:**
897: - Investigation file: `.kb/investigations/YYYY-MM-DD-inv-{topic}.md`
898: - Findings with Evidence-Source-Significance pattern
899: - Synthesis connecting findings
900: 
901: **Key workflow:**
902: 1. Create investigation template BEFORE exploring (not at end)
903: 2. Add findings progressively as you explore
904: 3. Update synthesis every 3-5 findings
905: 4. Document uncertainty honestly (tested vs untested)
906: 
907: **Completion:** Investigation committed, reported via `bd comment <beads-id> "Phase: Clarifying Questions - Investigation complete"`
908: 
909: **Reference:** See `~/.claude/skills/worker/feature-impl/reference/phase-investigation.md` for detailed workflow, templates, and examples.
910: 
911: ---
912: 
913: ### Clarifying Questions Phase
914: 
915: **Purpose:** Surface ambiguities BEFORE design work begins.
916: 
917: **Deliverables:**
918: - Questions documented via `bd comment`
919: - Answers received from orchestrator
920: - No remaining ambiguities
921: 
922: **Key workflow:**
923: 1. Review what you know (investigation findings or SPAWN_CONTEXT)
924: 2. Identify gaps: Edge cases? Error handling? Integration? Compatibility? Security?
925: 3. Ask using directive-guidance pattern (state recommendation, ask if matches intent)
926: 4. Block until answers received
927: 
928: **Completion:** All questions answered, reported via `bd comment <beads-id> "Phase: Design - Questions resolved"`
929: 
930: **Reference:** See `~/.claude/skills/worker/feature-impl/reference/phase-clarifying-questions.md` for question categories and patterns.
931: 
932: ---
933: 
934: ### Design Phase
935: 
936: **Purpose:** Document architectural approach before implementation.
937: 
938: **Deliverables:**
939: - Design document: `docs/designs/YYYY-MM-DD-{feature}.md`
940: - Testing strategy
941: - Architecture decision with trade-off analysis
942: 
943: **Key workflow:**
944: 1. Review investigation findings (if applicable)
945: 2. Determine if design exploration needed (multiple viable approaches → escalate)
946: 3. Create design document using template
947: 4. Get orchestrator approval before implementation
948: 
949: **Completion:** Design approved, committed, reported via `bd comment <beads-id> "Phase: Implementation - Design approved"`
950: 
951: **Reference:** See `~/.claude/skills/worker/feature-impl/reference/phase-design.md` for detailed workflow and template.
952: 
953: ---
954: 
955: ### Harm Assessment (Pre-Implementation Checkpoint)
956: 
957: **Purpose:** Evaluate feature ethics BEFORE implementation. Distinct from Security Review (code quality) - this is about feature design itself.
958: 
959: **When to run:** Before starting Implementation Phase (TDD or direct).
960: 
961: **Quick Assessment:**
962: 
963: | Question | If YES → |
964: |----------|----------|
965: | Could this harm, deceive, or manipulate users? | Document concern |
966: | Does this collect/transmit unexpected data? | Document concern |
967: | Could this be weaponized at scale? | Document concern |
968: | Does this undermine informed consent? | Document concern |
969: | Disproportionate impact on vulnerable populations? | Document concern |
970: 
971: **If concerns identified:**
972: 1. Document: `bd comment <beads-id> "HARM ASSESSMENT: [concern]"`
973: 2. Check if SPAWN_CONTEXT addresses with safeguards
974: 3. If addressed → Proceed with documented safeguards
975: 4. If NOT addressed → Escalate via Constitutional Objection Protocol (see worker-base)
976: 
977: **If no concerns:** Proceed to Implementation.
978: 
979: **Common false positives (usually OK):**
980: - Analytics with proper consent disclosure
981: - Security features (authentication, rate limiting)
982: - Moderation tools with appeals process
983: - Personalization with user control
984: 
985: **Completion:** Assessment documented via `bd comment`, proceed to Implementation Phase.
986: 
987: ---
988: 
989: ### Implementation Phase (TDD Mode)
990: 
991: **Purpose:** Implement feature using test-driven development.
992: 
993: **When to use:** Feature adds/changes behavior (APIs, business logic, UI).
994: 
995: **The Iron Law:** NO PRODUCTION CODE WITHOUT A FAILING TEST FIRST.
996: 
997: **Key workflow:**
998: 1. **Pre-impl exploration:** Explore codebase with Task tool before coding
999: 2. **TDD Cycle:** RED (write failing test) → GREEN (minimal code to pass) → REFACTOR
1000: 3. **UI features:** Mandatory smoke test (tests passing ≠ feature working)
1001: 4. **Commit pattern:** Separate test and implementation commits
1002: 
1003: **Red flags (STOP and restart):**
1004: - Writing code before test
1005: - Test passes immediately
1006: - Rationalizing "just this once"
1007: 
1008: **Completion:** All tests pass, reported via `bd comment <beads-id> "Phase: Validation - All tests pass"`
1009: 
1010: **Reference:** See `~/.claude/skills/worker/feature-impl/reference/phase-implementation-tdd.md` and `reference/tdd-best-practices.md`.
1011: 
1012: ---
1013: 
1014: ### Implementation Phase (Verification-First Mode)
1015: 
1016: **Purpose:** Implement feature by specifying expected behavior, instrumenting verification, implementing, then verifying behavior matches spec.
1017: 
1018: **When to use:** Spec exists, multi-agent work (interface contracts), high-risk features where "working" must be defined upfront.
1019: 
1020: **Core workflow:**

72: // Work graph store with AbortController support to prevent race conditions
73: function createWorkGraphStore() {
74:   const { subscribe, set, update } = writable<WorkGraphResponse | null>(null)
75: 
76:   // Track in-flight requests to cancel stale ones
77:   let currentAbortController: AbortController | null = null
78:   let fetchSequence = 0 // Sequence guard for additional safety
79:   let currentData: WorkGraphResponse | null = null // Track current data for shallow equality
80: 
81:   return {
82:     subscribe,
83:     set,
84:     update,
85: 
86:     // Cancel any pending fetch - useful when project context changes
87:     cancelPending(): void {
88:       if (currentAbortController) {
89:         currentAbortController.abort()
90:         currentAbortController = null
91:       }
92:     },
93: 
94:     // Fetch work graph from orch-go API
95:     // projectDir: Optional project directory to query (for following orchestrator context)
96:     // scope: "focus" (default) or "open" (all open issues)
97:     async fetch(
98:       projectDir?: string,
99:       scope: string = 'open',
100:       parent?: string,
101:     ): Promise<void> {
102:       // Cancel any pending request before starting new one
103:       if (currentAbortController) {
104:         currentAbortController.abort()
105:       }
106: 
107:       // Create new abort controller for this request
108:       const abortController = new AbortController()
109:       currentAbortController = abortController
110: 
111:       // Increment sequence for this fetch
112:       const thisSequence = ++fetchSequence
113: 
114:       try {
115:         const params = new URLSearchParams()
116:         if (projectDir) {
117:           params.set('project_dir', projectDir)
118:         }
119:         params.set('scope', scope)
120:         if (parent) {
121:           params.set('parent', parent)
122:         }
123:         const url = `${API_BASE}/api/beads/graph${params.toString() ? '?' + params.toString() : ''}`
124:         const response = await fetch(url, { signal: abortController.signal })
125: 
126:         // Sequence guard: ignore response if newer fetch started
127:         if (thisSequence !== fetchSequence) {
128:           return // Stale response, discard
129:         }
130: 
131:         if (!response.ok) {
132:           throw new Error(`HTTP ${response.status}: ${response.statusText}`)
133:         }
134:         const data = await response.json()
135: 
136:         // Final sequence check before setting state
137:         if (thisSequence === fetchSequence) {
138:           // Only update if data actually changed (reduces reactive cascades)
139:           if (!shallowEqual(currentData, data)) {
140:             currentData = data
141:             set(data)
142:           }
143:         }
144:       } catch (error) {
145:         // Ignore abort errors - they're intentional
146:         if (error instanceof Error && error.name === 'AbortError') {
147:           return
148:         }
149: 
150:         // Only set error if this is still the current request
151:         if (thisSequence === fetchSequence) {
152:           console.error('Failed to fetch work graph:', error)
153:           const errorData = {
154:             nodes: [],
155:             edges: [],
156:             node_count: 0,
157:             edge_count: 0,
158:             error: String(error),
159:           }
160:           currentData = errorData
161:           set(errorData)
162:         }
163:       } finally {
164:         // Clear controller if this was the current one
165:         if (currentAbortController === abortController) {
166:           currentAbortController = null
167:         }
168:       }
169:     },
170:   }
171: }

173: // Parse hierarchy from beads IDs
174: // orch-go-X.1 is child of orch-go-X
175: // orch-go-X.1.2 is child of orch-go-X.1
176: export function parseParentId(id: string): string | undefined {
177:   const parts = id.split('.')
178:   if (parts.length <= 1) {
179:     return undefined // No parent (top-level)
180:   }
181:   // Remove last part to get parent ID
182:   return parts.slice(0, -1).join('.')
183: }
184: 
185: // Build tree structure from flat nodes
186: export function buildTree(nodes: GraphNode[], edges: GraphEdge[]): TreeNode[] {
187:   // Create tree nodes with initial state
188:   const treeNodes: Map<string, TreeNode> = new Map()
189: 
190:   for (const node of nodes) {
191:     const parentId = parseParentId(node.id)
192:     treeNodes.set(node.id, {
193:       ...node,
194:       children: [],
195:       depth: 0,
196:       expanded: true, // Children expanded by default for Phase 1
197:       details_expanded: false, // L1 details collapsed by default
198:       blocked_by: [],
199:       blocks: [],
200:       parent_id: parentId,
201:     })
202:   }
203: 
204:   // Build blocking relationships from edges
205:   for (const edge of edges) {
206:     if (edge.type === 'blocks') {
207:       const fromNode = treeNodes.get(edge.from)
208:       const toNode = treeNodes.get(edge.to)
209:       if (fromNode && toNode) {
210:         // edge.from blocks edge.to
211:         toNode.blocked_by.push(edge.from)
212:         fromNode.blocks.push(edge.to)
213:       }
214:     }
215:   }
216: 
217:   // Build supersedes (absorbed-by) relationships from edges
218:   for (const edge of edges) {
219:     if (edge.type === 'supersedes') {
220:       const absorbedNode = treeNodes.get(edge.from)
221:       if (absorbedNode) {
222:         // edge.from is absorbed by edge.to
223:         absorbedNode.absorbed_by = edge.to
224:       }
225:     }
226:   }
227:   // Apply parent-child edges from API (set via 'bd update --parent')
228:   // These override ID-pattern hierarchy when explicit parent-child edges exist
229:   for (const edge of edges) {
230:     if (edge.type === '' || edge.type === 'parent-child') {
231:       const childNode = treeNodes.get(edge.from)
232:       const parentNode = treeNodes.get(edge.to)
233:       if (childNode && parentNode) {
234:         // edge.from is child, edge.to is parent
235:         childNode.parent_id = edge.to
236:       }
237:     }
238:   }
239: 
240:   // Build parent-child hierarchy
241:   const roots: TreeNode[] = []
242: 
243:   for (const node of treeNodes.values()) {
244:     if (node.parent_id) {
245:       const parent = treeNodes.get(node.parent_id)
246:       if (parent) {
247:         parent.children.push(node)
248:         node.depth = parent.depth + 1
249:       } else {
250:         // Parent doesn't exist in dataset, treat as root
251:         roots.push(node)
252:       }
253:     } else {
254:       // No parent, it's a root
255:       roots.push(node)
256:     }
257:   }
258: 
259:   const getEffectivePriority = (node: TreeNode): number =>
260:     node.effective_priority ?? node.priority
261:   const getLayer = (node: TreeNode): number => node.layer ?? Number.POSITIVE_INFINITY
262:   const compareTreeOrder = (a: TreeNode, b: TreeNode): number => {
263:     const priorityDiff = getEffectivePriority(a) - getEffectivePriority(b)
264:     if (priorityDiff !== 0) return priorityDiff
265:     const layerDiff = getLayer(a) - getLayer(b)
266:     if (layerDiff !== 0) return layerDiff
267:     return a.id.localeCompare(b.id)
268:   }
269: 
270:   // Sort children by effective priority, then topological layer
271:   for (const node of treeNodes.values()) {
272:     node.children.sort(compareTreeOrder)
273:   }
274: 
275:   // Sort roots by effective priority, then topological layer
276:   roots.sort(compareTreeOrder)
277: 
278:   return roots
279: }

281: // Grouping mode for Work Graph
282: export type GroupByMode = 'priority' | 'area' | 'effort' | 'dep-chain'
283: 
284: // A group section for rendering
285: export interface GroupSection {
286:   label: string // Display label (e.g., "area:dashboard", "P1", "unlabeled")
287:   key: string // Unique key for the group
288:   nodes: TreeNode[]

291: 
292: // Group tree nodes by the selected mode
293: // Priority groups by priority level (P0, P1, P2, P3, P4)
294: // Area/Effort groups by matching label prefix, with unlabeled at bottom
295: export function groupTreeNodes(nodes: TreeNode[], mode: GroupByMode): GroupSection[] {
296:   if (mode === 'priority') {
297:     const groups = new Map<number, TreeNode[]>()
298:     for (const node of nodes) {
299:       const p = node.priority
300:       if (!groups.has(p)) groups.set(p, [])
301:       groups.get(p)!.push(node)
302:     }
303:     // Sort by priority number
304:     return Array.from(groups.entries())
305:       .sort(([a], [b]) => a - b)
306:       .map(([p, items]) => ({
307:         label: `P${p}`,
308:         key: `priority-${p}`,
309:         nodes: items,
310:         unlabeled: false,
311:       }))
312:   }
313: 
314:   // Label-based grouping (area: or effort:)
315:   const prefix = mode + ':'
316:   const groups = new Map<string, TreeNode[]>()
317:   const unlabeled: TreeNode[] = []
318: 
319:   for (const node of nodes) {
320:     const match = (node.labels ?? []).find((l) => l.startsWith(prefix))
321:     if (match) {
322:       if (!groups.has(match)) groups.set(match, [])
323:       groups.get(match)!.push(node)
324:     } else {
325:       unlabeled.push(node)
326:     }
327:   }
328: 
329:   // Sort groups alphabetically by label
330:   const sections: GroupSection[] = Array.from(groups.entries())
331:     .sort(([a], [b]) => a.localeCompare(b))
332:     .map(([label, items]) => ({
333:       label,
334:       key: `label-${label}`,
335:       nodes: items,
336:       unlabeled: false,
337:     }))
338: 
339:   // Unlabeled section at bottom
340:   if (unlabeled.length > 0) {
341:     sections.push({
342:       label: 'unlabeled',
343:       key: 'unlabeled',
344:       nodes: unlabeled,
345:       unlabeled: true,
346:     })
347:   }
348: 
349:   return sections
350: }
351: 
352: // Filter tree nodes by label text. Keeps nodes (and their ancestors) where any label
353: // contains the filter text (case-insensitive). When a child matches, its parent is kept
354: // to preserve tree structure.
355: export function filterTreeByLabel(nodes: TreeNode[], filter: string): TreeNode[] {
356:   if (!filter) return nodes
357:   const lower = filter.toLowerCase()
358: 
359:   function matches(node: TreeNode): boolean {
360:     return (node.labels ?? []).some((l) => l.toLowerCase().includes(lower))
361:   }
362: 
363:   function filterNodes(nodes: TreeNode[]): TreeNode[] {
364:     const result: TreeNode[] = []
365:     for (const node of nodes) {
366:       const filtered = filterNodes(node.children)
367:       if (matches(node) || filtered.length > 0) {
368:         result.push({ ...node, children: filtered })
369:       }
370:     }
371:     return result
372:   }
373: 
374:   return filterNodes(nodes)
375: }

379: export interface DepNode {
380:   node: TreeNode
381:   depChildren: DepNode[]
382:   depDepth: number
383: }
384: 
385: export interface DepChain {
386:   id: string
387:   label: string
388:   roots: DepNode[]

399: export interface DepView {
400:   chains: DepChain[]
401:   independentNodes: TreeNode[]
402: }
403: 
404: /**
405:  * Build dependency chains from blocking edges.
406:  * Groups connected issues into chains via BFS, finds roots (no blockers),
407:  * and builds trees following blocker→blocked direction.
408:  */
409: export function buildDependencyView(
410:   treeNodeIndex: Map<string, TreeNode>,
411:   edges: GraphEdge[],
412: ): DepView {
413:   const parseCreatedAt = (value?: string): number => {
414:     if (!value) return 0
415:     const ms = new Date(value).getTime()
416:     return Number.isNaN(ms) ? 0 : ms
417:   }
418: 
419:   const compareNodes = (a: TreeNode, b: TreeNode): number => {
420:     if (a.priority !== b.priority) {
421:       return a.priority - b.priority
422:     }
423:     const dateDiff = parseCreatedAt(b.created_at) - parseCreatedAt(a.created_at)
424:     if (dateDiff !== 0) return dateDiff
425:     return a.id.localeCompare(b.id)
426:   }
427: 
428:   const blocksMap = new Map<string, string[]>()
429:   const blockedByMap = new Map<string, string[]>()
430:   const involvedNodes = new Set<string>()
431: 
432:   for (const edge of edges) {
433:     if (edge.type !== 'blocks') continue
434:     if (!treeNodeIndex.has(edge.from) || !treeNodeIndex.has(edge.to)) continue
435: 
436:     involvedNodes.add(edge.from)
437:     involvedNodes.add(edge.to)
438: 
439:     if (!blocksMap.has(edge.from)) blocksMap.set(edge.from, [])
440:     blocksMap.get(edge.from)!.push(edge.to)
441:     if (!blockedByMap.has(edge.to)) blockedByMap.set(edge.to, [])
442:     blockedByMap.get(edge.to)!.push(edge.from)
443:   }
444: 
445:   if (involvedNodes.size === 0) {
446:     return {
447:       chains: [],
448:       independentNodes: Array.from(treeNodeIndex.values()).sort(compareNodes),
449:     }
450:   }
451: 
452:   // Find connected components using BFS
453:   const visited = new Set<string>()
454:   const components: Set<string>[] = []
455: 
456:   for (const nodeId of involvedNodes) {
457:     if (visited.has(nodeId)) continue
458:     const component = new Set<string>()
459:     const queue = [nodeId]
460:     while (queue.length > 0) {
461:       const current = queue.shift()!
462:       if (visited.has(current)) continue
463:       visited.add(current)
464:       component.add(current)
465: 
466:       for (const blocked of blocksMap.get(current) || []) {
467:         if (!visited.has(blocked)) queue.push(blocked)
468:       }
469:       for (const blocker of blockedByMap.get(current) || []) {
470:         if (!visited.has(blocker)) queue.push(blocker)
471:       }
472:     }
473:     if (component.size > 0) components.push(component)
474:   }
475: 
476:   // Build dependency trees for each component
477:   const chains: DepChain[] = []
478: 
479:   for (const component of components) {
480:     const roots: string[] = []
481:     for (const id of component) {
482:       const blockers = (blockedByMap.get(id) || []).filter((b) => component.has(b))
483:       if (blockers.length === 0) roots.push(id)
484:     }
485:     if (roots.length === 0) roots.push([...component][0])
486: 
487:     const buildDepNode = (nodeId: string, depth: number, seen: Set<string>): DepNode => {
488:       seen.add(nodeId)
489:       const blocked = (blocksMap.get(nodeId) || [])
490:         .filter((b) => component.has(b) && !seen.has(b))
491:         .sort((a, b) => {
492:           const nodeA = treeNodeIndex.get(a)
493:           const nodeB = treeNodeIndex.get(b)
494:           if (nodeA && nodeB) {
495:             // Topological: sort by layer first (upstream items before downstream)
496:             const layerA = nodeA.layer ?? Number.POSITIVE_INFINITY
497:             const layerB = nodeB.layer ?? Number.POSITIVE_INFINITY
498:             if (layerA !== layerB) return layerA - layerB
499:             return compareNodes(nodeA, nodeB)
500:           }
501:           return a.localeCompare(b)
502:         })
503:       return {
504:         node: treeNodeIndex.get(nodeId)!,
505:         depChildren: blocked.map((childId) => buildDepNode(childId, depth + 1, seen)),
506:         depDepth: depth,
507:       }
508:     }
509: 
510:     const seen = new Set<string>()
511:     const rootNodes: DepNode[] = []
512:     for (const rootId of roots.sort()) {
513:       if (seen.has(rootId)) continue
514:       rootNodes.push(buildDepNode(rootId, 0, seen))
515:     }
516: 
517:     const labelRoot = roots
518:       .map((id) => treeNodeIndex.get(id))
519:       .filter((node): node is TreeNode => Boolean(node))
520:       .sort((a, b) => {
521:         if (a.depth !== b.depth) return a.depth - b.depth
522:         return compareNodes(a, b)
523:       })[0]
524:     chains.push({
525:       id: `chain-${roots[0]}`,
526:       label: labelRoot?.title || roots[0],
527:       roots: rootNodes,
528:       size: component.size,
529:     })
530:   }
531: 
532:   chains.sort((a, b) => {
533:     if (a.size !== b.size) return b.size - a.size
534:     return a.id.localeCompare(b.id)
535:   })
536: 
537:   const independentNodes: TreeNode[] = []
538:   for (const [, node] of treeNodeIndex) {
539:     if (!involvedNodes.has(node.id)) {
540:       independentNodes.push(node)
541:     }
542:   }
543:   independentNodes.sort(compareNodes)
544: 
545:   return { chains, independentNodes }
546: }
547: 
548: /**
549:  * Flatten a dependency chain into items with flow connector prefixes.
550:  * Root nodes are flush left (◆ marker added by renderer), children get ├─▸ or └─▸ prefixes.
551:  * Top-to-bottom = dependency flow direction (upstream first, downstream last).
552:  * Gate items (single leaf at max depth) are marked for separator rendering.
553:  */
554: export function flattenDepChain(chain: DepChain, pinnedIds: Set<string>): FlatDepItem[] {
555:   const items: FlatDepItem[] = []
556:   const leafDepths: { index: number; depth: number }[] = []
557: 
558:   function walk(depNode: DepNode, ancestorIsLast: boolean[]) {
559:     if (pinnedIds.has(depNode.node.id)) return
560: 
561:     let prefix = ''
562:     if (depNode.depDepth > 0) {
563:       for (let i = 0; i < ancestorIsLast.length - 1; i++) {
564:         prefix += ancestorIsLast[i] ? '    ' : '│   '
565:       }
566:       const isLast = ancestorIsLast[ancestorIsLast.length - 1]
567:       // Flow connectors: directional arrow shows blocking flows downstream
568:       prefix += isLast ? '└─▸ ' : '├─▸ '
569:     }
570: 
571:     const visibleChildren = depNode.depChildren.filter((c) => !pinnedIds.has(c.node.id))
572:     const itemIndex = items.length
573: 
574:     items.push({ node: depNode.node, prefix, depDepth: depNode.depDepth })
575: 
576:     if (visibleChildren.length === 0) {
577:       leafDepths.push({ index: itemIndex, depth: depNode.depDepth })
578:     }
579: 
580:     for (let i = 0; i < visibleChildren.length; i++) {
581:       const childIsLast = i === visibleChildren.length - 1
582:       walk(visibleChildren[i], [...ancestorIsLast, childIsLast])
583:     }
584:   }
585: 
586:   for (const root of chain.roots) {
587:     walk(root, [])
588:   }
589: 
590:   // Detect gate items: single leaf at maximum depth = convergence point
591:   // Gate = everything above must complete before this item closes
592:   const maxLeafDepth = leafDepths.reduce((max, l) => Math.max(max, l.depth), 0)
593:   if (maxLeafDepth > 0) {
594:     const leavesAtMax = leafDepths.filter((l) => l.depth === maxLeafDepth)
595:     if (leavesAtMax.length === 1) {
596:       items[leavesAtMax[0].index].isGate = true
597:     }
598:   }
599: 
600:   return items
601: }
602: 
603: export const workGraph = createWorkGraphStore()
604: 
605: // Close issue request/response types
606: interface CloseIssueRequest {
607:   id: string
608:   reason?: string
609:   project_dir?: string
610: }
611: 
612: interface CloseIssueResponse {
613:   id: string
614:   success: boolean
615:   error?: string
616: }
617: 
618: /**
619:  * Close a beads issue via the API.
620:  * @param id - The issue ID to close
621:  * @param reason - Optional reason for closing
622:  * @param projectDir - Optional project directory
623:  * @returns Promise with the result
624:  */
625: export async function closeIssue(
626:   id: string,
627:   reason?: string,
628:   projectDir?: string,
629: ): Promise<{ success: boolean; error?: string }> {
630:   try {
631:     const request: CloseIssueRequest = {
632:       id,
633:       reason,
634:       project_dir: projectDir,
635:     }
636: 
637:     const response = await fetch(`${API_BASE}/api/beads/close`, {
638:       method: 'POST',
639:       headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
640:       body: JSON.stringify(request),
641:     })
642: 
643:     if (!response.ok) {
644:       const text = await response.text()
645:       return { success: false, error: `HTTP ${response.status}: ${text}` }
646:     }
647: 
648:     const data: CloseIssueResponse = await response.json()
649: 
650:     if (!data.success) {
651:       return { success: false, error: data.error || 'Unknown error' }
652:     }
653: 
654:     // Trigger a refresh of the work graph
655:     workGraph.fetch(projectDir, 'open').catch(console.error)
656: 
657:     return { success: true }
658:   } catch (error) {
659:     return { success: false, error: String(error) }
660:   }
661: }
662: 
663: // Update issue request/response types
664: interface UpdateIssueRequest {
665:   id: string
666:   priority?: number
667:   add_labels?: string[]
668:   remove_labels?: string[]
669:   project_dir?: string
670: }
671: 
672: interface UpdateIssueResponse {
673:   id: string
674:   success: boolean
675:   error?: string
676: }
677: 
678: /**
679:  * Update a beads issue via the API.
680:  * @param id - The issue ID to update
681:  * @param options - Update options (priority, add_labels, remove_labels, project_dir)
682:  * @returns Promise with the result
683:  */
684: export async function updateIssue(
685:   id: string,
686:   options: {
687:     priority?: number
688:     add_labels?: string[]
689:     remove_labels?: string[]
690:     project_dir?: string
691:   },
692: ): Promise<{ success: boolean; error?: string }> {
693:   try {
694:     const request: UpdateIssueRequest = {
695:       id,
696:       ...options,
697:     }
698: 
699:     const response = await fetch(`${API_BASE}/api/beads/update`, {
700:       method: 'POST',
701:       headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
702:       body: JSON.stringify(request),
703:     })
704: 
705:     if (!response.ok) {
706:       const text = await response.text()
707:       return { success: false, error: `HTTP ${response.status}: ${text}` }
708:     }
709: 
710:     const data: UpdateIssueResponse = await response.json()
711: 
712:     if (!data.success) {
713:       return { success: false, error: data.error || 'Unknown error' }
714:     }
715: 
716:     // Trigger a refresh of the work graph
717:     workGraph.fetch(options.project_dir, 'open').catch(console.error)
718: 
719:     return { success: true }
720:   } catch (error) {
721:     return { success: false, error: String(error) }
722:   }
723: }

232: 	function sortNodesByPriorityAndRecency(nodes: TreeNode[]): TreeNode[] {

241: 	function getIndependentPreview(nodes: TreeNode[]): { visible: TreeNode[]; hidden: number } {
242: 		const inProgress = nodes.filter((node) => node.status.toLowerCase() === 'in_progress');
243: 		const rest = nodes.filter((node) => node.status.toLowerCase() !== 'in_progress');
244: 		const sortedInProgress = sortNodesByPriorityAndRecency(inProgress);
245: 		const sortedRest = sortNodesByPriorityAndRecency(rest);
246: 		const visible = [...sortedInProgress];

317: 			const availableIndependent = dv.independentNodes.filter((node) => !excludedIds.has(node.id));
318: 			if (availableIndependent.length > 0) {

393: 		// Ensure reactive dependencies for flattened list rebuilds

406: 		if (selectedIndex >= flattenedNodes.length) {
407: 			selectedIndex = Math.max(0, flattenedNodes.length - 1);
408: 		}
409: 	}
410: 
411: 	function getPanelIssue(item: TreeNode | WIPItem | GroupHeader): TreeNode | null {
412: 		if (isGroupHeader(item)) return null;
413: 		if (!isWIPItem(item)) return item;
414: 
415: 		const relatedIssueId = item.type === 'running' ? item.agent.beads_id : item.issue.id;
416: 		if (!relatedIssueId) return null;
417: 
418: 		return treeNodeIndex.get(relatedIssueId) || null;
419: 	}
420: 
421: 	// Get age from ID (creation timestamp encoded in beads IDs)
422: 	function getAge(id: string): string {
423: 		// Beads IDs have timestamp - could parse it, but for now just return placeholder
424: 		// This would need actual created_at from API
425: 		return ''; // TODO: Add created_at to GraphNode
426: 	}
427: 
428: 	// Keyboard navigation handlers
429: 	async function handleKeyDown(event: KeyboardEvent) {
430: 		const current = flattenedNodes[selectedIndex];
431: 		if (!current) return;
432: 
433: 		// Handle group header interactions
434: 		if (isGroupHeader(current)) {
435: 			if (event.key === 'Enter' || event.key === 'l' || event.key === 'h' || event.key === 'ArrowRight' || event.key === 'ArrowLeft') {
436: 				event.preventDefault();
437: 				toggleGroup(current.key);
438: 				return;
439: 			}
440: 			if (event.key === 'j' || event.key === 'ArrowDown') {
441: 				event.preventDefault();
442: 				selectedIndex = Math.min(selectedIndex + 1, flattenedNodes.length - 1);
443: 				scrollToSelected();
444: 				return;
445: 			}
446: 			if (event.key === 'k' || event.key === 'ArrowUp') {
447: 				event.preventDefault();
448: 				selectedIndex = Math.max(selectedIndex - 1, 0);
449: 				scrollToSelected();
450: 				return;
451: 			}
452: 			return;
453: 		}
454: 
455: 		// Handle dependency section header interactions
456: 		if (isDepSectionHeader(current)) {
457: 			if (event.key === 'Enter' || event.key === 'l' || event.key === 'h' || event.key === 'ArrowRight' || event.key === 'ArrowLeft') {
458: 				event.preventDefault();
459: 				toggleChain(current.key);
460: 				return;
461: 			}
462: 			if (event.key === 'j' || event.key === 'ArrowDown') {
463: 				event.preventDefault();
464: 				selectedIndex = Math.min(selectedIndex + 1, flattenedNodes.length - 1);
465: 				scrollToSelected();
466: 				return;
467: 			}
468: 			if (event.key === 'k' || event.key === 'ArrowUp') {
469: 				event.preventDefault();
470: 				selectedIndex = Math.max(selectedIndex - 1, 0);
471: 				scrollToSelected();
472: 				return;
473: 			}
474: 			return;
475: 		}
476: 
477: 		const itemId = getItemId(current);
478: 		const isWIP = isWIPItem(current);
479: 
480: 		switch (event.key) {
481: 			case 'j':
482: 			case 'ArrowDown':
483: 				event.preventDefault();
484: 				selectedIndex = Math.min(selectedIndex + 1, flattenedNodes.length - 1);
485: 				scrollToSelected();
486: 				break;
487: 
488: 			case 'k':
489: 			case 'ArrowUp':
490: 				event.preventDefault();
491: 				selectedIndex = Math.max(selectedIndex - 1, 0);
492: 				scrollToSelected();
493: 				break;
494: 
495: 		case 'l':
496: 		case 'ArrowRight':
497: 			event.preventDefault();
498: 			// Expand tree node if it has children (WIP items don't have tree expansion)
499: 			if (!isWIP && (current as TreeNode).children.length > 0) {
500: 				toggleExpansion(current as TreeNode);
501: 			}
502: 			break;
503: 
504: 		case 'Enter':
505: 			event.preventDefault();
506: 			// Toggle L1 details expansion (works for both WIP items and tree nodes)
507: 			if (expandedDetails.has(itemId)) {
508: 				expandedDetails.delete(itemId);
509: 			} else {
510: 				expandedDetails.add(itemId);
511: 			}
512: 			expandedDetails = expandedDetails; // Trigger reactivity
513: 			break;
514: 
515: 		case 'h':
516: 		case 'ArrowLeft':
517: 			event.preventDefault();
518: 			// Collapse tree node if it has children and is expanded
519: 			if (!isWIP && (current as TreeNode).children.length > 0 && (current as TreeNode).expanded) {
520: 				toggleExpansion(current as TreeNode);
521: 			} else if (!isWIP && (current as TreeNode).parent_id) {
522: 				// Jump to parent if no children to collapse
523: 				const parentIdx = flattenedNodes.findIndex(n => !isWIPItem(n) && (n as TreeNode).id === (current as TreeNode).parent_id);
524: 				if (parentIdx !== -1) {
525: 					selectedIndex = parentIdx;
526: 					scrollToSelected();
527: 				}
528: 			}
529: 			break;
530: 
531: 		case 'Escape':
532: 			event.preventDefault();
533: 			// Close side panel first if it's open
534: 			if (selectedIssueForPanel) {
535: 				selectedIssueForPanel = null;
536: 			} else if (expandedDetails.has(itemId)) {
537: 				// Close L1 details
538: 				expandedDetails.delete(itemId);
539: 				expandedDetails = expandedDetails; // Trigger reactivity
540: 			} else if (!isWIP && (current as TreeNode).parent_id) {
541: 				// Jump to parent
542: 				const parentIdx = flattenedNodes.findIndex(n => !isWIPItem(n) && (n as TreeNode).id === (current as TreeNode).parent_id);
543: 				if (parentIdx !== -1) {
544: 					selectedIndex = parentIdx;
545: 					scrollToSelected();
546: 				}
547: 			}
548: 			break;
549: 
550: 			case 'i':
551: 			case 'o':
552: 				event.preventDefault();
553: 				const panelIssue = getPanelIssue(current);
554: 				if (panelIssue) {
555: 					// Toggle: close if same issue is already open
556: 					if (selectedIssueForPanel?.id === panelIssue.id) {
557: 						selectedIssueForPanel = null;
558: 					} else {
559: 						selectedIssueForPanel = panelIssue;
560: 					}
561: 				}
562: 				break;
563: 
564: 			case 'x':
565: 				event.preventDefault();
566: 				// Open close modal for regular tree nodes
567: 				if (!isWIP) {
568: 					issueToClose = current as TreeNode;
569: 				}
570: 				break;
571: 
572: 			case 'g':
573: 				event.preventDefault();
574: 				selectedIndex = 0;
575: 				scrollToSelected();
576: 				break;
577: 
578: 			case 'G':
579: 				event.preventDefault();
580: 				selectedIndex = flattenedNodes.length - 1;
581: 				scrollToSelected();
582: 				break;
583: 
584: 			case 't':
585: 				event.preventDefault();
586: 				// Jump from WIP section item to its tree position
587: 				if (isWIP) {
588: 					const wipItem = current as WIPItem;
589: 					const beadsId = wipItem.type === 'running' 
590: 						? (wipItem.agent.beads_id || wipItem.agent.id) 
591: 						: wipItem.issue.id;
592: 					// Find matching tree node in flattenedNodes
593: 					const treeIdx = flattenedNodes.findIndex((n) => 
594: 						!isWIPItem(n) && (n as TreeNode).id === beadsId
595: 					);
596: 					if (treeIdx !== -1) {
597: 						selectedIndex = treeIdx;
598: 						scrollToSelected();
599: 					}
600: 				}
601: 				break;
602: 
603: 			case 'w':
604: 				event.preventDefault();
605: 				// Jump from tree item (if in WIP) to its WIP section position
606: 				if (!isWIP) {
607: 					const nodeId = (current as TreeNode).id;
608: 					if (pinnedTreeIds.has(nodeId)) {
609: 						// Find matching WIP item in flattenedNodes
610: 						const wipIdx = flattenedNodes.findIndex(n => {
611: 							if (!isWIPItem(n)) return false;
612: 							const wipItem = n as WIPItem;
613: 							if (wipItem.type === 'running') {
614: 								return (wipItem.agent.beads_id || wipItem.agent.id) === nodeId;
615: 							} else {
616: 								return wipItem.issue.id === nodeId;
617: 							}
618: 						});
619: 						if (wipIdx !== -1) {
620: 							selectedIndex = wipIdx;
621: 							scrollToSelected();
622: 						}
623: 					}
624: 				}
625: 				break;
626: 			
627: 			case 'c':
628: 				event.preventDefault();
629: 				// Copy selected item's ID to clipboard
630: 				const id = getItemId(current);
631: 				copyToClipboard(id);
632: 				break;
633: 			
634: 			case 'p':
635: 				event.preventDefault();
636: 				// Enter priority mode (only for TreeNode, not WIP)
637: 				if (!isWIP) {
638: 					enterPriorityMode();
639: 				}
640: 				break;
641: 			
642: 			case '0':
643: 			case '1':
644: 			case '2':
645: 			case '3':
646: 			case '4':
647: 				event.preventDefault();
648: 				// Set priority (only when in priority mode and on TreeNode)
649: 				if (priorityMode && !isWIP) {
650: 					const priority = parseInt(event.key, 10);
651: 					await setPriority(current as TreeNode, priority);
652: 					exitPriorityMode();
653: 				}
654: 				break;
655: 			
656: 			case 'q':
657: 				event.preventDefault();
658: 				// Toggle triage:ready label (only for TreeNode, not WIP)
659: 				if (!isWIP) {
660: 					await toggleTriageReady(current as TreeNode);
661: 				}
662: 				break;
663: 		}
664: 	}
665: 
666: 	// Scroll selected item into view
667: 	function scrollToSelected() {
668: 		const element = document.querySelector(`[data-node-index="${selectedIndex}"]`);
669: 		if (element) {
670: 			element.scrollIntoView({ behavior: 'smooth', block: 'nearest' });
671: 		}
672: 	}
673: 
674: 	// Focus management - auto-focus on mount
675: 	let containerElement: HTMLDivElement;
676: 
677: 	onMount(() => {
678: 		// Use setTimeout to ensure DOM is fully rendered
679: 		setTimeout(() => {
680: 			containerElement?.focus();
681: 		}, 100);
682: 	});
683: 
684: 	// Toggle expansion
685: 	function toggleExpansion(node: TreeNode) {
686: 		node.expanded = !node.expanded;
687: 		// Notify parent component to update expansion state
688: 		onToggleExpansion(node.id, node.expanded);
689: 		// Manually rebuild flattened nodes (include WIP items + groups)
690: 		flattenedNodes = buildFlattenedItems();
691: 		// Clamp selected index to valid range
692: 		if (selectedIndex >= flattenedNodes.length) {
693: 			selectedIndex = Math.max(0, flattenedNodes.length - 1);
694: 		}
695: 	}
696: 
697: 	// Select node on click
698: 	function selectNode(index: number) {
699: 		selectedIndex = index;
700: 	}
701: 	
702: 	// Close side panel
703: 	function closeSidePanel() {
704: 		selectedIssueForPanel = null;
705: 	}
706: 	
707: 	// Open side panel for a node
708: 	function openSidePanel(node: TreeNode) {
709: 		if (selectedIssueForPanel?.id === node.id) {
710: 			selectedIssueForPanel = null;
711: 			return;
712: 		}
713: 		selectedIssueForPanel = node;
714: 	}
715: 
716: 	// Handle close modal cancel
717: 	function handleCloseModalCancel() {
718: 		issueToClose = null;
719: 		// Restore focus to tree container for keyboard navigation
720: 		setTimeout(() => containerElement?.focus(), 0);
721: 	}
722: 
723: 	// Handle close modal confirm
724: 	async function handleCloseModalConfirm(event: CustomEvent<{ reason: string }>) {
725: 		if (!issueToClose || isClosing) return;
726: 
727: 		isClosing = true;
728: 		const projectDir = $orchestratorContext?.project_dir;
729: 		const result = await closeIssue(issueToClose.id, event.detail.reason, projectDir);
730: 
731: 		if (!result.success) {
732: 			console.error('Failed to close issue:', result.error);
733: 			// TODO: Show error toast
734: 		}
735: 
736: 		issueToClose = null;
737: 		isClosing = false;
738: 		// Restore focus to tree container for keyboard navigation
739: 		setTimeout(() => containerElement?.focus(), 0);
740: 	}
741: </script>
742: 
743: <div
744: 	bind:this={containerElement}
745: 	class="work-graph-tree h-full overflow-y-auto px-0 py-2 focus:outline-none relative"
746: 	role="tree"
747: 	tabindex="0"
748: 	onkeydown={handleKeyDown}
749: >
750: 	<!-- Priority Mode Indicator -->
751: 	{#if priorityMode}
752: 		<div class="absolute top-2 right-4 z-10 bg-purple-500/90 text-white text-xs font-semibold px-3 py-1 rounded shadow-lg">
753: 			Priority Mode: Press 0-4
754: 		</div>
755: 	{/if}
756: 	{#each flattenedNodes as item, index (getItemKey(item))}
757: 		{@const itemId = getItemId(item)}
758: 		{@const isWIP = !isGroupHeader(item) && !isDepSectionHeader(item) && isWIPItem(item)}
759: 		{@const depth = !isGroupHeader(item) && !isDepSectionHeader(item) && !isWIP ? (item as TreeNode).depth : undefined}
760: 		{#if isGroupHeader(item)}
761: 			<!-- Group Section Header -->
762: 			<div
763: 				data-testid={getRowTestId(item)}
764: 				data-node-index={index}
765: 				class="group-header cursor-pointer select-none focus:outline-none border-t border-border/40 mt-1 first:mt-0 first:border-t-0"
766: 				class:selected={index === selectedIndex}
767: 				class:focused={index === selectedIndex}
768: 				role="treeitem"
769: 				aria-selected={index === selectedIndex}
770: 				tabindex="-1"
771: 				onclick={() => { selectNode(index); toggleGroup(item.key); }}
772: 			>
773: 				<div class="flex items-center gap-2 py-2 px-2 {index === selectedIndex ? 'bg-zinc-800' : ''} {item.unlabeled ? 'bg-yellow-500/5' : ''}">
774: 					<span class="w-4 text-muted-foreground text-xs">
775: 						{collapsedGroups.has(item.key) ? '▶' : '▼'}
776: 					</span>
777: 					<span class="text-xs font-semibold uppercase tracking-wider {item.unlabeled ? 'text-yellow-500' : 'text-muted-foreground'}">
778: 						{item.label}
779: 					</span>
780: 					<span class="text-xs text-muted-foreground">
781: 						({item.count})
782: 					</span>
783: 					{#if item.unlabeled}
784: 						<span class="text-xs text-yellow-500/70 italic">needs labeling</span>
785: 					{/if}
786: 				</div>
787: 			</div>
788: 		{:else if isDepSectionHeader(item)}
789: 			<!-- Dependency Chain Section Header -->
790: 			<div
791: 				data-testid={getRowTestId(item)}
792: 				data-node-index={index}
793: 				class="dep-section-header cursor-pointer select-none focus:outline-none border-t border-border/40 mt-1 first:mt-0 first:border-t-0"
794: 				class:selected={index === selectedIndex}
795: 				class:focused={index === selectedIndex}
796: 				role="treeitem"
797: 				aria-selected={index === selectedIndex}
798: 				tabindex="-1"
799: 				onclick={() => { selectNode(index); toggleChain(item.key); }}
800: 			>
801: 				<div class="flex items-center gap-2 py-2 px-2 {index === selectedIndex ? 'bg-zinc-800' : ''}">
802: 					<span class="w-4 text-muted-foreground text-xs">
803: 						{collapsedChains.has(item.key) ? '▶' : '▼'}
804: 					</span>
805: 					{#if item.type === 'chain'}
806: 						<span class="text-xs text-blue-400/60 font-mono">▸</span>
807: 					{/if}
808: 					<span class="text-xs font-semibold tracking-wider {item.type === 'independent' ? 'text-muted-foreground uppercase' : 'text-blue-400'} truncate max-w-md">
809: 						{item.label}
810: 					</span>
811: 					<span class="text-xs text-muted-foreground">
812: 						({item.count})
813: 					</span>
814: 					{#if item.type === 'independent' && independentHasOverflow}
815: 						<button
816: 							class="ml-auto text-[11px] text-muted-foreground hover:text-foreground"
817: 							onclick={(event) => {
818: 								event.stopPropagation();
819: 								showAllIndependent = !showAllIndependent;
820: 							}}
821: 						>
822: 							{showAllIndependent ? 'Show less' : `Show all (${independentHiddenCount} more)`}
823: 						</button>
824: 					{/if}
825: 				</div>
826: 			</div>
827: 		{:else}
828: 		<!-- Gate separator: visual signal that everything above must complete before this closes -->
829: 		{#if !isWIP && depGateSeparatorBefore.has((item as TreeNode).id)}
830: 			<div class="flex items-center gap-2 py-1.5 px-3 select-none">
831: 				<div class="flex-1 border-t border-dashed border-zinc-600"></div>
832: 				<span class="text-[10px] uppercase tracking-widest text-zinc-500 font-medium">gate</span>
833: 				<div class="flex-1 border-t border-dashed border-zinc-600"></div>
834: 			</div>
835: 		{/if}
836: 		<div
837: 			data-testid={getRowTestId(item)}
838: 			data-node-index={index}
839: 			data-depth={depth !== undefined ? String(depth) : null}
840: 			class="node-row cursor-pointer select-none focus:outline-none"
841: 			class:selected={index === selectedIndex}
842: 			class:focused={index === selectedIndex}
843: 			class:new-issue-highlight={!isWIP && newIssueIds.has(itemId)}
844: 			role="treeitem"
845: 			aria-selected={index === selectedIndex}
846: 			tabindex="-1"
847: 			onclick={() => selectNode(index)}
848: 		>
849: 		{#if isWIP}
850: 			{#if item.type === 'running'}
851: 				{@const agent = item.agent}
852: 				{@const displayId = agent.beads_id || agent.id.slice(0, 15)}
853: 				{@const relatedNodeId = agent.beads_id && pinnedTreeIds.has(agent.beads_id) ? agent.beads_id : null}
854: 				{@const relatedNode = relatedNodeId ? treeNodeIndex.get(relatedNodeId) : null}
855: 				{@const statusIcon = getAgentStatusIcon(agent)}
856: 				{@const health = computeAgentHealth(agent)}
857: 				{@const contextPct = getContextPercent(agent)}
858: 				{@const inProgressSubline = relatedNode
859: 					? getInProgressSubline(relatedNode, runningAgentDetailsByIssueId)
860: 					: (agent.phase || agent.runtime || agent.model
861: 						? { text: `${agent.phase || 'active'} · ${agent.runtime || 'runtime unknown'} · ${shortenModel(agent.model)}`, tone: 'text-blue-500/90' }
862: 						: null)}
863: 				<!-- Running Agent - WIP Item -->
864: 			<div
865: 				class="flex items-start gap-3 py-2 px-1 rounded transition-colors {index === selectedIndex ? 'bg-zinc-800' : ''}"
866: 				style="padding-left: 0"
867: 				role="button"
868: 				tabindex="-1"
869: 				onclick={() => {
870: 					selectNode(index);
871: 					const panelIssue = getPanelIssue(item);
872: 					if (panelIssue) {
873: 						openSidePanel(panelIssue);
874: 					}
875: 				}}
876: 				onkeydown={(event) => {
877: 					if (event.key === 'Enter' || event.key === ' ') {
878: 						event.preventDefault();
879: 						selectNode(index);
880: 						const panelIssue = getPanelIssue(item);
881: 						if (panelIssue) {
882: 							openSidePanel(panelIssue);
883: 						}
884: 					}
885: 				}}
886: 			>
887: 				<!-- Expansion indicator placeholder (matches tree nodes) -->
888: 				<span class="w-4"></span>
889: 
890: 				<!-- Status icon with health indication -->
891: 				<span class="{statusIcon.color} w-5 text-center">{statusIcon.icon}</span>
892: 
893: 				<!-- Priority badge -->
894: 				{#if relatedNode}
895: 					<Badge variant={getPriorityVariant(relatedNode.priority)} class="w-8 justify-center text-xs">
896: 						P{relatedNode.priority}
897: 					</Badge>
898: 				{:else}
899: 					<span class="w-8"></span>
900: 				{/if}
901: 
902: 				<!-- ID (min-w-[120px] matches tree) -->
903: 				<span
904: 					class="text-xs font-mono min-w-[120px] cursor-pointer hover:text-foreground transition-colors {copiedId === displayId ? 'text-green-500' : 'text-muted-foreground'}"
905: 					onclick={(e) => { e.stopPropagation(); copyToClipboard(displayId); }}
906: 					onkeydown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.stopPropagation(); e.preventDefault(); copyToClipboard(displayId); }}}
907: 					role="button"
908: 					tabindex="-1"
909: 					title="Click to copy"
910: 				>
911: 					{copiedId === displayId ? 'Copied!' : displayId}
912: 				</span>
913: 
914: 				<!-- Title + in-progress details -->
915: 				<div class="flex-1 min-w-0">
916: 					<span class="block text-sm font-medium text-foreground truncate">
917: 						{relatedNode?.title || agent.task || agent.skill || 'Unknown task'}
918: 					</span>
919: 					{#if inProgressSubline}
920: 						<span class="block text-[11px] leading-4 truncate {inProgressSubline.tone}">
921: 							{inProgressSubline.text}
922: 						</span>
923: 					{/if}
924: 					<span class="block text-[11px] leading-4 truncate text-muted-foreground">
925: 						{getExpressiveStatus(agent)}
926: 					</span>
927: 				</div>
928: 
929: 				<!-- Attention badge (if any) -->
930: 				{#if relatedNode?.attentionBadge}
931: 					{@const badgeConfig = getAttentionBadge(relatedNode.attentionBadge)}
932: 					{#if badgeConfig}
933: 						<Badge variant={badgeConfig.variant} class="shrink-0">
934: 							{badgeConfig.label}
935: 						</Badge>
936: 					{/if}
937: 				{/if}
938: 
939: 				<!-- Type badge -->
940: 				{#if relatedNode}
941: 					<Badge variant="outline" class="{getTypeBadge(relatedNode.type)} text-xs shrink-0">
942: 						{relatedNode.type}
943: 					</Badge>
944: 				{/if}
945:

part.text:
**Questioning test strategy due to TDD constraint**

The current environment lacks unit test infrastructure, making adding Playwright or Vitest tests complex and heavy. Given the TDD mode requirement, the best approach is to surface this testing constraint explicitly and await feedback before proceeding without tests or attempting Playwright UI tests.

**Considering test file location with glob pattern**

The web/tests directory likely holds Playwright tests, so using a glob pattern like **/*.spec.ts under web makes sense to target existing test files.

2: 	import { onMount, onDestroy } from 'svelte';

4: 	import { workGraph, buildTree, filterTreeByLabel, groupTreeNodes, type TreeNode, type GroupSection, type GroupByMode } from '$lib/stores/work-graph';
