6: 	import { kbModelProbes } from '$lib/stores/kb-model-probes';
7: 	import { orchestratorContext } from '$lib/stores/context';
8: 	import { agents, connectSSE, disconnectSSE, sseEvents, connectionStatus, type Agent } from '$lib/stores/agents';
9: 	import {
10: 		agentlogEvents,
11: 		connectAgentlogSSE,
12: 		disconnectAgentlogSSE,
13: 		type AgentLogEvent,
14: 	} from '$lib/stores/agentlog';
15: 	import { WorkGraphTree } from '$lib/components/work-graph-tree';
16: 	import { ViewToggle } from '$lib/components/view-toggle';
17: 	import { GroupByDropdown } from '$lib/components/group-by-dropdown';
18: 	import { LabelFilter } from '$lib/components/label-filter';
19: 	import { ArtifactFeed } from '$lib/components/artifact-feed';
20: 	import { RecentlyCompletedSection } from '$lib/components/recently-completed-section';
21: 	import { wip, wipItems } from '$lib/stores/wip';
22: 	import { daemon, type DaemonStatus } from '$lib/stores/daemon';
23: 	import { attention, formatRelativeTime, type CompletedIssue } from '$lib/stores/attention';
24: 	import { focus, type FocusInfo } from '$lib/stores/focus';
25: 
26: 	const WORK_GRAPH_POLL_INTERVAL_MS = 30000;
27: 	const WORK_GRAPH_MAX_BACKOFF_MS = 120000;
28: 	const CONTEXT_POLL_INTERVAL_MS = 15000;
29: 	const EVENT_DRIVEN_REFRESH_THROTTLE_MS = 3000;
30: 	const EVENT_DRIVEN_REFRESH_TYPES = new Set([
31: 		'session.created',
32: 		'session.deleted',
33: 		'agent.completed',
34: 		'agent.abandoned',
35: 	]);
36: 	const AGENTLOG_EVENT_DRIVEN_REFRESH_TYPES = new Set([
37: 		'session.spawned',
38: 		'session.completed',
39: 		'session.error',
40: 		'session.auto_completed',
41: 		'agent.completed',
42: 		'agent.abandoned',
43: 	]);
44: 	
45: 	// Derived store for project_dir to isolate reactivity
46: 	// Only triggers reactive blocks when project_dir changes, not other context fields
47: 	const projectDir = derived(orchestratorContext, $ctx => $ctx.project_dir);
48: 
49: 	// Per-project seen issues tracking to prevent false highlights on project switch
50: 	const SEEN_ISSUES_KEY = 'work-graph-seen-issues';
51: 	
52: 	interface SeenIssuesState {
53: 		byProject: Record<string, {
54: 			issueIds: string[];
55: 			firstSeenAt: string; // ISO timestamp
56: 		}>;
57: 	}
58: 	
59: 	function loadSeenIssues(): SeenIssuesState {
60: 		if (typeof window === 'undefined') return { byProject: {} };
61: 		try {
62: 			const stored = localStorage.getItem(SEEN_ISSUES_KEY);
63: 			if (stored) {
64: 				return JSON.parse(stored);
65: 			}
66: 		} catch (e) {
67: 			console.error('Failed to load seen issues from localStorage:', e);
68: 		}
69: 		return { byProject: {} };
70: 	}
71: 	
72: 	function saveSeenIssues(state: SeenIssuesState): void {
73: 		if (typeof window === 'undefined') return;
74: 		try {
75: 			localStorage.setItem(SEEN_ISSUES_KEY, JSON.stringify(state));
76: 		} catch (e) {
77: 			console.error('Failed to save seen issues to localStorage:', e);
78: 		}
79: 	}
80: 
81: 	let tree: TreeNode[] = [];
82: 	let loading = true;
83: 	let error: string | null = null;
84: 	let currentView: 'issues' | 'artifacts' | 'completed' = 'issues';
85: 	let refreshTimeout: ReturnType<typeof setTimeout> | null = null;
86: 	let isRefreshCycleInFlight = false;
87: 	let isRefreshPolling = false;
88: 	let refreshBackoffMs = WORK_GRAPH_POLL_INTERVAL_MS;
89: 	let lastEventDrivenRefreshAt = 0;
90: 	let lastProcessedSSEEventId: string | null = null;
91: 	let lastProcessedAgentlogEventId: string | null = null;
92: 	let agentlogRealtimeStartUnix = 0;
93: 	let seenIssuesState: SeenIssuesState = { byProject: {} };
94: 	let currentProjectDir: string | undefined = undefined;
95: 	let projectChangeDebounceTimeout: ReturnType<typeof setTimeout> | null = null;
96: 	let previousIssueIds = new Set<string>();
97: 	let newIssueIds = new Set<string>();
98: 	// Flag to prevent marking all issues as "new" on first load
99: 	// Set to true after onMount initializes previousIssueIds
100: 	let isNewIssueDetectionEnabled = false;
101: 	let completedIssues: CompletedIssue[] = [];
102: 	let focusedBeadsId: string | undefined = undefined; // Current focus beads ID for auto-scoping
103: 	let labelFilter: string = '';
104: 	let labelFilterComponent: { focus: () => void };
105: 	let groupByMode: GroupByMode = 'priority';
106: 
107: 	interface ReadyToCompleteItem {
108: 		id: string;
109: 		title: string;
110: 		type: string;
111: 		priority: number;
112: 		runtime?: string;
113: 		tokenTotal: number | null;
114: 		completionAt: string;
115: 	}
116: 
117: 	let readyToCompleteItems: ReadyToCompleteItem[] = [];
118: 	let readyToCompleteIds = new Set<string>();
119: 	
120: 	// Persist groupBy mode in localStorage
121: 	const GROUP_BY_KEY = 'work-graph-group-by';
122: 	if (typeof window !== 'undefined') {
123: 		const stored = localStorage.getItem(GROUP_BY_KEY);
124: 		if (stored === 'priority' || stored === 'area' || stored === 'effort' || stored === 'dep-chain') {
125: 			groupByMode = stored;
126: 		}
127: 	}
128: 	
129: 	// Track expansion state separately to preserve across tree rebuilds
130: 	let expansionState = new Map<string, boolean>();
131: 	
132: 	// Debounce timeout for tree rebuild to batch rapid store updates
133: 	let rebuildDebounceTimeout: ReturnType<typeof setTimeout> | null = null;
134: 	let hasRenderedTree = false; // Skip debounce until first tree render completes
135: 
136: 	async function runRefreshCycle(): Promise<boolean> {
137: 		const projectDir = $orchestratorContext?.project_dir;
138: 
139: 		const requests: Promise<void>[] = [
140: 			workGraph.fetch(projectDir, 'open', focusedBeadsId),
141: 			wip.fetchQueued(projectDir),
142: 			daemon.fetch(),
143: 			attention.fetch(projectDir),
144: 			agents.fetch(), // Refresh agents to detect phase transitions (Phase: Complete via bd comment)
145: 		];
146: 
147: 		if (currentView === 'artifacts' && $kbArtifacts) {
148: 			requests.push(kbArtifacts.fetch(projectDir));
149: 		}
150: 
151: 		if (currentView === 'artifacts' && $kbModelProbes) {
152: 			requests.push(kbModelProbes.fetch(projectDir));
153: 		}
154: 
155: 		const results = await Promise.allSettled(requests);
156: 		return results.every((result) => result.status === 'fulfilled');
157: 	}
158: 
159: 	function scheduleNextRefresh(delayMs: number): void {
160: 		if (!isRefreshPolling || refreshTimeout) return;
161: 
162: 		refreshTimeout = setTimeout(async () => {
163: 			refreshTimeout = null;
164: 			if (!isRefreshPolling) return;
165: 
166: 			if (isRefreshCycleInFlight) {
167: 				scheduleNextRefresh(refreshBackoffMs);
168: 				return;
169: 			}
170: 
171: 			isRefreshCycleInFlight = true;
172: 			try {
173: 				const ok = await runRefreshCycle();
174: 				if (ok) {
175: 					refreshBackoffMs = WORK_GRAPH_POLL_INTERVAL_MS;
176: 				} else {
177: 					refreshBackoffMs = Math.min(refreshBackoffMs * 2, WORK_GRAPH_MAX_BACKOFF_MS);
178: 				}
179: 			} finally {
180: 				isRefreshCycleInFlight = false;
181: 				scheduleNextRefresh(refreshBackoffMs);
182: 			}
183: 		}, delayMs);
184: 	}
185: 
186: 	function startRefreshPolling(): void {
187: 		if (isRefreshPolling) return;
188: 		isRefreshPolling = true;
189: 		refreshBackoffMs = WORK_GRAPH_POLL_INTERVAL_MS;
190: 		scheduleNextRefresh(refreshBackoffMs);
191: 	}
192: 
193: 	async function triggerEventDrivenRefresh(): Promise<void> {
194: 		if (!isRefreshPolling || isRefreshCycleInFlight) return;
195: 
196: 		const now = Date.now();
197: 		if (now - lastEventDrivenRefreshAt < EVENT_DRIVEN_REFRESH_THROTTLE_MS) {
198: 			return;
199: 		}
200: 		lastEventDrivenRefreshAt = now;
201: 
202: 		if (refreshTimeout) {
203: 			clearTimeout(refreshTimeout);
204: 			refreshTimeout = null;
205: 		}
206: 
207: 		isRefreshCycleInFlight = true;
208: 		try {
209: 			const ok = await runRefreshCycle();
210: 			if (ok) {
211: 				refreshBackoffMs = WORK_GRAPH_POLL_INTERVAL_MS;
212: 			} else {
213: 				refreshBackoffMs = Math.min(refreshBackoffMs * 2, WORK_GRAPH_MAX_BACKOFF_MS);
214: 			}
215: 		} finally {
216: 			isRefreshCycleInFlight = false;
217: 			scheduleNextRefresh(refreshBackoffMs);
218: 		}
219: 	}
220: 
221: 	// Fetch work graph and agents on mount, connect to SSE for real-time updates
222: 	onMount(async () => {
223: 		// Load seen issues from localStorage
224: 		seenIssuesState = loadSeenIssues();
225: 		
226: 		// Start orchestratorContext polling (slower cadence to avoid backend saturation)
227: 		orchestratorContext.startPolling(CONTEXT_POLL_INTERVAL_MS);
228: 
229: 		const projectDir = $orchestratorContext?.project_dir;
230: 		currentProjectDir = projectDir;
231: 		
232: 		// Fetch focus first to get the beads_id for auto-scoping
233: 		await focus.fetch();
234: 		const focusBeadsId = $focus?.beads_id;
235: 		focusedBeadsId = focusBeadsId;
236: 		
237: 		await Promise.all([
238: 			workGraph.fetch(projectDir, 'open', focusBeadsId),
239: 			agents.fetch(),
240: 			attention.fetch(projectDir) // Fetch attention signals and completed issues (filtered by project)
241: 		]);
242: 
243: 		// Fetch WIP and daemon data (non-blocking)
244: 		wip.fetchQueued(projectDir).catch(console.error);
245: 		daemon.fetch().catch(console.error);
246: 		
247: 		loading = false;
248: 		
249: 		// Initialize previousIssueIds from stored state OR initial fetch
250: 		if (projectDir && seenIssuesState.byProject[projectDir]) {
251: 			// Use stored state for this project
252: 			previousIssueIds = new Set(seenIssuesState.byProject[projectDir].issueIds);
253: 		} else if ($workGraph?.nodes) {
254: 			// First time seeing this project - store all current issues as "seen"
255: 			previousIssueIds = new Set($workGraph.nodes.map(n => n.id));
256: 			if (projectDir) {
257: 				seenIssuesState.byProject[projectDir] = {
258: 					issueIds: Array.from(previousIssueIds),
259: 					firstSeenAt: new Date().toISOString()
260: 				};
261: 				saveSeenIssues(seenIssuesState);
262: 			}
263: 		}
264: 		
265: 		// Enable new issue detection now that previousIssueIds is initialized
266: 		isNewIssueDetectionEnabled = true;
267: 
268: 		// Connect to SSE for real-time agent updates (WIP section)
269: 		connectSSE();
270: 		agentlogRealtimeStartUnix = Math.floor(Date.now() / 1000);
271: 		connectAgentlogSSE();
272: 
273: 		// Keep a low-frequency poll as fallback; rely on SSE for responsive refreshes
274: 		startRefreshPolling();
275: 	});
276: 
277: 	$: if ($sseEvents.length > 0 && !loading) {
278: 		const latestEvent = $sseEvents[$sseEvents.length - 1];
279: 		if (latestEvent.id !== lastProcessedSSEEventId) {
280: 			lastProcessedSSEEventId = latestEvent.id;
281: 			if (EVENT_DRIVEN_REFRESH_TYPES.has(latestEvent.type)) {
282: 				triggerEventDrivenRefresh().catch(console.error);
283: 			}
284: 		}
285: 	}
286: 
287: 	$: if ($agentlogEvents.length > 0 && !loading) {
288: 		const latestAgentlogEvent = $agentlogEvents[$agentlogEvents.length - 1] as AgentLogEvent;
289: 		if (latestAgentlogEvent.id !== lastProcessedAgentlogEventId) {
290: 			lastProcessedAgentlogEventId = latestAgentlogEvent.id;
291: 			if (
292: 				latestAgentlogEvent.timestamp >= agentlogRealtimeStartUnix &&
293: 				AGENTLOG_EVENT_DRIVEN_REFRESH_TYPES.has(latestAgentlogEvent.type)
294: 			) {
295: 				triggerEventDrivenRefresh().catch(console.error);
296: 			}
297: 		}
298: 	}
299: 
300: 	// Subscribe to focus changes and update focusedBeadsId for auto-scoping
301: 	$: if ($focus?.beads_id) {
302: 		focusedBeadsId = $focus.beads_id;
303: 	} else {
304: 		focusedBeadsId = undefined;
305: 	}
306: 
307: 	// Sync running agents from agents store to WIP store
308: 	$: wip.setRunningAgents($agents);
309: 
310: 	// Disconnect SSE and stop polling on unmount
311: 	onDestroy(() => {
312: 		disconnectSSE();
313: 		disconnectAgentlogSSE();
314: 		orchestratorContext.stopPolling();
315: 		isRefreshPolling = false;
316: 		if (refreshTimeout) {
317: 			clearTimeout(refreshTimeout);
318: 			refreshTimeout = null;
319: 		}
320: 		isRefreshCycleInFlight = false;
321: 		if (projectChangeDebounceTimeout) {
322: 			clearTimeout(projectChangeDebounceTimeout);
323: 			projectChangeDebounceTimeout = null;
324: 		}
325: 		if (rebuildDebounceTimeout) {
326: 			clearTimeout(rebuildDebounceTimeout);
327: 			rebuildDebounceTimeout = null;
328: 		}
329: 		// Cancel any pending workGraph fetches
330: 		workGraph.cancelPending();
331: 	});
332: 
333: 	// Subscribe to attention store for completed issues
334: 	$: if ($attention) {
335: 		completedIssues = $attention.completedIssues;
336: 	}
337: 
338: 	// Build a dedicated review queue for agents that reported Phase: Complete
339: 	// while their beads issue is still in_progress (not closed yet).
340: 	$: {
341: 		const queueByIssue = new Map<string, ReadyToCompleteItem>();
342: 		const nodesById = new Map(($workGraph?.nodes || []).map((node) => [node.id, node]));
343: 
344: 		for (const agent of $agents || []) {
345: 			const beadsId = agent.beads_id;
346: 			if (!beadsId) continue;
347: 			// Include 'completed' status because determineAgentStatus() returns 'completed'
348: 			// for Phase: Complete agents whose sessions are still alive (Priority 3 in cascade).
349: 			// Closed issues are excluded below via the issueNode.status check (workGraph scope=open
350: 			// means closed issues aren't in the graph at all).
351: 			if (agent.status !== 'active' && agent.status !== 'awaiting-cleanup' && agent.status !== 'completed') continue;
352: 			if (agent.phase?.toLowerCase() !== 'complete') continue;
353: 
354: 			const issueNode = nodesById.get(beadsId);
355: 			if (!issueNode || issueNode.source !== 'beads') continue;
356: 			if (issueNode.status.toLowerCase() !== 'in_progress') continue;
357: 
358: 			const completionAt = agent.phase_reported_at || agent.updated_at || agent.spawned_at;
359: 			if (!completionAt) continue;
360: 
361: 			const candidate: ReadyToCompleteItem = {
362: 				id: beadsId,
363: 				title: issueNode.title,
364: 				type: issueNode.type,
365: 				priority: issueNode.priority,
366: 				runtime: agent.runtime,
367: 				tokenTotal: getAgentTokenTotal(agent),
368: 				completionAt,
369: 			};
370: 
371: 			const existing = queueByIssue.get(beadsId);
372: 			if (!existing || completionMs(candidate) > completionMs(existing)) {
373: 				queueByIssue.set(beadsId, candidate);
374: 			}
375: 		}
376: 
377: 		readyToCompleteItems = Array.from(queueByIssue.values()).sort((a, b) => {
378: 			const ageDiff = completionMs(a) - completionMs(b); // oldest completion first
379: 			if (ageDiff !== 0) return ageDiff;
380: 			if (a.priority !== b.priority) return a.priority - b.priority;
381: 			return a.id.localeCompare(b.id);
382: 		});
383: 	}
384: 
385: 	$: {
386: 		readyToCompleteIds = new Set(readyToCompleteItems.map((item) => item.id));
387: 	}
388: 
389: 
390: 	// Rebuild tree and phases whenever graph data OR attention changes
391: 	// Debounced to batch rapid updates and reduce CPU during polling
392: 	// Skip debounce until first tree render completes for immediate display
393: 	$: if ($workGraph && !$workGraph.error) {
394: 		// Cancel any pending rebuild
395: 		if (rebuildDebounceTimeout) {
396: 			clearTimeout(rebuildDebounceTimeout);
397: 		}
398: 		
399: 		// Debounce rebuild to batch rapid updates (50ms is fast but still batches)
400: 		const executeRebuild = () => {
401: 			rebuildDebounceTimeout = null;
402: 
403: 			// Build tree from full open set
404: 			tree = buildTree($workGraph.nodes, $workGraph.edges);
405: 			
406: 			// Mark that we've completed first render (enable debouncing for subsequent updates)
407: 			hasRenderedTree = true;
408: 
409: 			// Apply stored expansion state to preserve user's collapse/expand choices
410: 			const applyExpansionState = (nodes: TreeNode[]) => {
411: 				for (const node of nodes) {
412: 					// If we have stored expansion state for this node, apply it
413: 					// Otherwise keep the default from buildTree (which is expanded: true)
414: 					if (expansionState.has(node.id)) {
415: 						node.expanded = expansionState.get(node.id)!;
416: 					} else {
417: 						// First time seeing this node, store its default state
418: 						expansionState.set(node.id, node.expanded);
419: 					}
420: 					// Recursively apply to children
421: 					if (node.children.length > 0) {
422: 						applyExpansionState(node.children);
423: 					}
424: 				}
425: 			};
426: 			applyExpansionState(tree);
427: 
428: 			// Attach attention badges to tree nodes
429: 			if ($attention?.signals) {
430: 				const attachBadges = (nodes: TreeNode[]) => {
431: 					for (const node of nodes) {
432: 						const signal = $attention.signals.get(node.id);
433: 						if (signal) {
434: 							node.attentionBadge = signal.badge;
435: 							node.attentionReason = signal.reason;
436: 						}
437: 						if (node.children.length > 0) {
438: 							attachBadges(node.children);
439: 						}
440: 					}
441: 				};
442: 				attachBadges(tree);
443: 			}
444: 
445: 			error = null;
446: 			
447: 			// Track newly appeared issues for highlighting (use API nodes, not filtered nodes)
448: 			// This prevents highlighting children when expanding parents (they were always in the API data)
449: 			if ($workGraph.nodes) {
450: 				const currentIssueIds = new Set($workGraph.nodes.map(n => n.id));
451: 				const projectDir = $orchestratorContext?.project_dir;
452: 				
453: 				// Find issues that are new (in current but not in previous)
454: 				// Only detect new issues after onMount has initialized previousIssueIds
455: 				// This prevents marking all issues as "new" on first load
456: 				for (const id of currentIssueIds) {
457: 					if (isNewIssueDetectionEnabled && !previousIssueIds.has(id) && !newIssueIds.has(id)) {
458: 						newIssueIds.add(id);
459: 						newIssueIds = newIssueIds; // Trigger reactivity
460: 						// Remove highlight after 30 seconds
461: 						setTimeout(() => {
462: 							newIssueIds.delete(id);
463: 							newIssueIds = newIssueIds; // Trigger reactivity
464: 						}, 30000);
465: 					}
466: 				}
467: 				
468: 				// Update previousIssueIds for next comparison
469: 				previousIssueIds = currentIssueIds;
470: 				
471: 				// Persist seen issues to localStorage for this project
472: 				if (projectDir) {
473: 					const existingFirstSeen = seenIssuesState.byProject[projectDir]?.firstSeenAt;
474: 					seenIssuesState.byProject[projectDir] = {
475: 						issueIds: Array.from(currentIssueIds),
476: 						firstSeenAt: existingFirstSeen || new Date().toISOString()
477: 					};
478: 					saveSeenIssues(seenIssuesState);
479: 				}
480: 			}
481: 		};
482: 		
483: 		// Execute immediately until first tree render, then debounce subsequent updates
484: 		if (hasRenderedTree) {
485: 			rebuildDebounceTimeout = setTimeout(executeRebuild, 50); // 50ms batches rapid updates
486: 		} else {
487: 			executeRebuild(); // Immediate for first render
488: 		}
489: 	} else if ($workGraph?.error) {
490: 		error = $workGraph.error;
491: 		tree = [];
492: 	}
493: 	
494: 	// Re-fetch workGraph and kbArtifacts when orchestrator project_dir changes
495: 	// Uses derived store to isolate reactivity (only fires when project_dir changes)
496: 	// Uses debounce + abort to prevent flip-flopping between old/new project data
497: 	$: {
498: 		if (typeof window !== 'undefined' && $projectDir) {
499: 			const newProjectDir = $projectDir;
500: 			
501: 			// Only react to actual project changes (not other context changes)
502: 			if (newProjectDir !== currentProjectDir) {
503: 				// Cancel any pending debounced fetch
504: 				if (projectChangeDebounceTimeout) {
505: 					clearTimeout(projectChangeDebounceTimeout);
506: 				}
507: 				
508: 				// Cancel any in-flight workGraph requests immediately
509: 				workGraph.cancelPending();
510: 				
511: 				// Update state synchronously to prevent stale comparisons
512: 				currentProjectDir = newProjectDir;
513: 				
514: 				// Clear current highlights - they belong to the old project
515: 				newIssueIds = new Set<string>();
516: 				
517: 				// Load seen issues for this project from localStorage
518: 				if (seenIssuesState.byProject[newProjectDir]) {
519: 					previousIssueIds = new Set(seenIssuesState.byProject[newProjectDir].issueIds);
520: 				} else {
521: 					// New project we haven't seen before - will be populated on first fetch
522: 					previousIssueIds = new Set<string>();
523: 				}
524: 				
525: 				// Debounce the actual fetch to wait for stable project value
526: 				// 300ms prevents rapid flip-flopping while still feeling responsive
527: 				projectChangeDebounceTimeout = setTimeout(() => {
528: 					projectChangeDebounceTimeout = null;
529: 					workGraph.fetch(newProjectDir, 'open', focusedBeadsId).catch(console.error);
530: 					attention.fetch(newProjectDir).catch(console.error); // Re-fetch attention for new project
531: 					// Also re-fetch kbArtifacts if we're in artifacts view
532: 					if (currentView === 'artifacts' && $kbArtifacts) {
533: 						kbArtifacts.fetch(newProjectDir).catch(console.error);
534: 					}
535: 					if (currentView === 'artifacts' && $kbModelProbes) {
536: 						kbModelProbes.fetch(newProjectDir).catch(console.error);
537: 					}
538: 				}, 300);
539: 			}
540: 		}
541: 	}
542: 
543: 	// Handle view toggle
544: 	async function handleViewToggle(view: 'issues' | 'artifacts' | 'completed') {
545: 		currentView = view;
546: 		
547: 		// Fetch artifacts when switching to artifacts view
548: 		if (view === 'artifacts') {
549: 			const projectDir = $orchestratorContext?.project_dir;
550: 			const requests: Promise<void>[] = [];
551: 			if (!$kbArtifacts) {
552: 				requests.push(kbArtifacts.fetch(projectDir));
553: 			}
554: 			if (!$kbModelProbes) {
555: 				requests.push(kbModelProbes.fetch(projectDir));
556: 			}
557: 			if (requests.length > 0) {
558: 				await Promise.all(requests);
559: 			}
560: 		}
561: 	}
562: 
563: 	// Manual retry handler
564: 	async function handleRetry() {
565: 		await orchestratorContext.retry();
566: 	}
567: 
568: 	// Handle expansion state updates from tree component
569: 	function handleToggleExpansion(nodeId: string, expanded: boolean) {
570: 		expansionState.set(nodeId, expanded);
571: 	}
572: 
573: 	// Keyboard navigation for Tab to toggle views
574: 	// Handle clear focus button click
575: 	async function handleClearFocus() {
576: 		const result = await focus.clearFocus();
577: 		if (result.success) {
578: 			// Refresh the work graph without focus filter
579: 			focusedBeadsId = undefined;
580: 			const projectDir = $orchestratorContext?.project_dir;
581: 			workGraph.fetch(projectDir, 'open').catch(console.error);
582: 		} else {
583: 			console.error('Failed to clear focus:', result.error);
584: 		}
585: 	}
586: 
587: 	// Handle setting focus on an epic
588: 	async function handleSetFocus(beadsId: string, title: string) {
589: 		const result = await focus.setFocus(title, beadsId);
590: 		if (result.success) {
591: 			// Update local state and refresh graph with new focus
592: 			focusedBeadsId = beadsId;
593: 			const projectDir = $orchestratorContext?.project_dir;
594: 			workGraph.fetch(projectDir, 'open', beadsId).catch(console.error);
595: 		} else {
596: 			console.error('Failed to set focus:', result.error);
597: 		}
598: 	}
599: 
600: 	// Cycle views: issues -> completed -> artifacts -> issues
601: 	const viewOrder: ('issues' | 'completed' | 'artifacts')[] = ['issues', 'completed', 'artifacts'];
602: 
603: 	function handleKeydown(event: KeyboardEvent) {
604: 		// Don't cycle views when a side panel dialog is open (panel handles Tab)
605: 		if (event.key === 'Tab' && !document.querySelector('[role="dialog"]')) {
606: 			event.preventDefault();
607: 			const idx = viewOrder.indexOf(currentView);
608: 			if (event.shiftKey) {
609: 				currentView = viewOrder[(idx - 1 + viewOrder.length) % viewOrder.length];
610: 			} else {
611: 				currentView = viewOrder[(idx + 1) % viewOrder.length];
612: 			}
613: 			handleViewToggle(currentView);
614: 		}
615: 		// '/' to focus label filter (like GitHub)
616: 		if (event.key === '/' && currentView === 'issues') {
617: 			const active = document.activeElement;
618: 			if (active?.tagName !== 'INPUT' && active?.tagName !== 'TEXTAREA') {
619: 				event.preventDefault();
620: 				labelFilterComponent?.focus();
621: 			}
622: 		}
623: 		// 'G' (shift+g) to cycle group mode when in issues view
624: 		if (event.key === 'G' && event.shiftKey && currentView === 'issues') {
625: 			const active = document.activeElement;
626: 			if (active?.tagName !== 'INPUT' && active?.tagName !== 'TEXTAREA') {
627: 				event.preventDefault();
628: 				event.stopPropagation();
629: 				const idx = groupOrder.indexOf(groupByMode);
630: 				handleGroupByChange(groupOrder[(idx + 1) % groupOrder.length]);
631: 			}
632: 		}
633: 	}
634: 	
635: 	// Get help text based on current view mode
636: 	// Compute filtered tree whenever tree or labelFilter changes
637: 	$: filteredTree = labelFilter ? filterTreeByLabel(tree, labelFilter) : tree;
638: 
639: 	// Compute group sections from filtered tree
640: 	$: groupSections = (groupByMode === 'area' || groupByMode === 'effort')
641: 		? groupTreeNodes(filteredTree, groupByMode)
642: 		: [] as GroupSection[];
643: 
644: 	function handleLabelFilterChange(value: string) {
645: 		labelFilter = value;
646: 	}
647: 
648: 	function handleGroupByChange(mode: GroupByMode) {
649: 		groupByMode = mode;
650: 		if (typeof window !== 'undefined') {
651: 			localStorage.setItem(GROUP_BY_KEY, mode);
652: 		}
653: 	}
654: 
655: 	function getAgentTokenTotal(agent: Agent): number | null {
656: 		const tokens = agent.tokens;
657: 		if (!tokens) return null;
658: 
659: 		const total =
660: 			tokens.total_tokens ??
661: 			(tokens.input_tokens || 0) +
662: 				(tokens.output_tokens || 0) +
663: 				(tokens.cache_read_tokens || 0);
664: 
665: 		if (!Number.isFinite(total) || total <= 0) {
666: 			return null;
667: 		}
668: 
669: 		return total;
670: 	}
671: 
672: 	function formatTokenTotal(total: number | null): string {
673: 		if (total === null) return 'tokens unknown';
674: 		if (total >= 1_000_000) return `${(total / 1_000_000).toFixed(1)}M tokens`;
675: 		if (total >= 1_000) return `${(total / 1_000).toFixed(1)}k tokens`;
676: 		return `${total} tokens`;
677: 	}
678: 
679: 	function completionMs(item: ReadyToCompleteItem): number {
680: 		const ms = new Date(item.completionAt).getTime();
681: 		if (Number.isNaN(ms)) return 0;
682: 		return ms;
683: 	}
684: 
685: 	// Cycle group mode order for 'g' shortcut
686: 	const groupOrder: GroupByMode[] = ['priority', 'area', 'effort', 'dep-chain'];
687: 
688: 	function getHelpText(): string {
689: 		if (currentView === 'completed') {
690: 			return 'Completed view - Navigate with j/k, details with enter, verify with v, needs fix with x';
691: 		}
692: 		if (currentView === 'artifacts') {
693: 			return 'Artifact view - Navigate with j/k, open with l/enter, Tab to toggle';
694: 		}
695: 		return 'Tree view - Navigate with j/k, expand with l/enter, collapse with h/esc, close with x';
696: 	}
697: 
698: 	function daemonQueueSummary(status: DaemonStatus): string {
699: 		const queued = status.queue?.queued ?? status.ready_count ?? 0;
700: 		const reasons: string[] = [];
701: 		if ((status.queue?.waiting_for_slots ?? 0) > 0) {
702: 			reasons.push(`${status.queue?.waiting_for_slots} waiting for slots`);
703: 		}
704: 		if ((status.queue?.grace_period ?? 0) > 0) {
705: 			reasons.push(`${status.queue?.grace_period} in grace period`);
706: 		}
707: 		if ((status.queue?.processed_cache ?? 0) > 0) {
708: 			reasons.push(`${status.queue?.processed_cache} in processed cache`);
709: 		}
710: 
711: 		if (queued === 0 || reasons.length === 0) {
712: 			return `${queued} queued`;
713: 		}
714: 
715: 		return `${queued} queued (${reasons.join(', ')})`;
716: 	}
717: </script>

867: 										<span class="text-muted-foreground whitespace-nowrap">{item.runtime || 'runtime unknown'}</span>

870: 									</div>

875: 
876: 					{#if filteredTree.length > 0}

879: 								tree={filteredTree}
880: 								groups={groupSections}

882: 								edges={$workGraph?.edges || []}

I'm checking the getRowTestId helper that provides data-testid for work-graph tree items to understand how the test IDs are assigned.

<type>file</type>
<content>1: import { ATTENTION_BADGE_CONFIG } from '$lib/stores/attention'
2: import type { AttentionBadgeType, TreeNode } from '$lib/stores/work-graph'
3: import { computeAgentHealth, type WIPItem } from '$lib/stores/wip'
4: 
5: export interface GroupHeader {
6:   _groupHeader: true
7:   key: string
8:   label: string
9:   count: number
10:   unlabeled: boolean
11: }
12: 
13: export interface DepSectionHeader {
14:   _depSectionHeader: true
15:   key: string
16:   label: string
17:   count: number
18:   type: 'chain' | 'independent'
19: }
20: 
21: export type RunningAgentDetails = {
22:   phase?: string
23:   runtime?: string
24:   model?: string
25:   skill?: string
26: }
27: 
28: export function getAttentionBadge(
29:   badge: AttentionBadgeType | 'unverified' | 'needs_fix' | undefined,
30: ) {
31:   if (!badge) return null
32:   return ATTENTION_BADGE_CONFIG[badge] || null
33: }
34: 
35: export function isGroupHeader(item: unknown): item is GroupHeader {
36:   return !!item && typeof item === 'object' && '_groupHeader' in item
37: }
38: 
39: export function isDepSectionHeader(item: unknown): item is DepSectionHeader {
40:   return !!item && typeof item === 'object' && '_depSectionHeader' in item
41: }
42: 
43: export function isWIPItem(item: TreeNode | WIPItem | GroupHeader): item is WIPItem {
44:   return 'type' in item && (item.type === 'running' || item.type === 'queued')
45: }
46: 
47: export function getItemId(item: TreeNode | WIPItem | GroupHeader | DepSectionHeader): string {
48:   if (isGroupHeader(item)) return item.key
49:   if (isDepSectionHeader(item)) return item.key
50:   if (isWIPItem(item)) {
51:     return item.type === 'running' ? item.agent.id : item.issue.id
52:   }
53:   return item.id
54: }
55: 
56: export function getItemKey(item: TreeNode | WIPItem | GroupHeader | DepSectionHeader): string {
57:   if (isGroupHeader(item)) return `group-${item.key}`
58:   if (isDepSectionHeader(item)) return `dep-${item.key}`
59:   if (isWIPItem(item)) {
60:     return item.type === 'running'
61:       ? `wip-running-${item.agent.id}`
62:       : `wip-queued-${item.issue.id}`
63:   }
64:   return `tree-${item.id}`
65: }
66: 
67: export function getRowTestId(item: TreeNode | WIPItem | GroupHeader | DepSectionHeader): string {
68:   if (isGroupHeader(item)) return `group-header-${item.key}`
69:   if (isDepSectionHeader(item)) return `dep-header-${item.key}`
70:   if (isWIPItem(item)) {
71:     return item.type === 'running'
72:       ? `wip-row-${item.agent.beads_id || item.agent.id}`
73:       : `wip-row-${item.issue.id}`
74:   }
75:   return `issue-row-${item.id}`
76: }
77: 
78: export function flattenTree(nodes: TreeNode[], result: TreeNode[] = []): TreeNode[] {
79:   for (const node of nodes) {
80:     result.push(node)
81:     if (node.expanded && node.children.length > 0) {
82:       flattenTree(node.children, result)
83:     }
84:   }
85:   return result
86: }
87: 
88: export function flattenVisibleTree(
89:   nodes: TreeNode[],
90:   pinnedIds: Set<string>,
91: ): TreeNode[] {
92:   return flattenTree(nodes).filter((node) => !pinnedIds.has(node.id))
93: }
94: 
95: export function findNodeById(nodes: TreeNode[], nodeId: string): TreeNode | null {
96:   for (const node of nodes) {
97:     if (node.id === nodeId) {
98:       return node
99:     }
100:     if (node.children.length > 0) {
101:       const childMatch = findNodeById(node.children, nodeId)
102:       if (childMatch) {
103:         return childMatch
104:       }
105:     }
106:   }
107:   return null
108: }
109: 
110: export function getStatusIcon(status: string): string {
111:   switch (status.toLowerCase()) {
112:     case 'in_progress':
113:       return '▶'
114:     case 'blocked':
115:       return '🚫'
116:     case 'open':
117:       return '○'
118:     case 'closed':
119:       return '✓'
120:     case 'complete':
121:       return '✓'
122:     default:
123:       return '•'
124:   }
125: }
126: 
127: export function getStatusColor(status: string): string {
128:   switch (status.toLowerCase()) {
129:     case 'in_progress':
130:       return 'text-blue-500'
131:     case 'blocked':
132:       return 'text-red-500'
133:     case 'open':
134:       return 'text-muted-foreground'
135:     case 'closed':
136:       return 'text-green-500'
137:     case 'complete':
138:       return 'text-green-500'
139:     default:
140:       return 'text-muted-foreground'
141:   }
142: }
143: 
144: export function formatStatusLabel(status: string): string {
145:   return status.replace(/_/g, ' ')
146: }
147: 
148: export function isDoneStatus(status: string): boolean {
149:   const normalized = status.toLowerCase()
150:   return normalized === 'closed' || normalized === 'complete' || normalized === 'accepted'
151: }
152: 
153: function normalizeDescription(description?: string): string {
154:   return description?.replace(/\s+/g, ' ').trim() || ''
155: }
156: 
157: function truncateText(text: string, limit: number): string {
158:   if (text.length <= limit) {
159:     return text
160:   }
161:   return `${text.slice(0, Math.max(0, limit - 3)).trimEnd()}...`
162: }
163: 
164: export function getIssueSummary(node: TreeNode): string {
165:   const description = normalizeDescription(node.description)
166:   if (description) {
167:     const sentenceMatch = description.match(/^(.+?[.!?])(\s|$)/)
168:     const sentence = sentenceMatch ? sentenceMatch[1] : description
169:     return truncateText(sentence, 160)
170:   }
171: 
172:   if (node.status.toLowerCase() === 'in_progress') {
173:     return 'Work is currently active and waiting for the next phase transition.'
174:   }
175:   if (node.blocked_by.length > 0) {
176:     return 'This issue is waiting on upstream work before execution can continue.'
177:   }
178:   if (node.blocks.length > 0) {
179:     return 'This issue is a dependency for downstream work and unblocks others when complete.'
180:   }
181:   return 'No summary provided yet. Expand related issues below for context.'
182: }
183: 
184: function collectDescendants(node: TreeNode): TreeNode[] {
185:   const descendants: TreeNode[] = []
186:   const stack = [...node.children]
187:   while (stack.length > 0) {
188:     const next = stack.shift()
189:     if (!next) continue
190:     descendants.push(next)
191:     if (next.children.length > 0) {
192:       stack.push(...next.children)
193:     }
194:   }
195:   return descendants
196: }
197: 
198: function countVisibleDescendants(node: TreeNode): number {
199:   if (!node.expanded || node.children.length === 0) {
200:     return 0
201:   }
202: 
203:   let visible = 0
204:   for (const child of node.children) {
205:     visible += 1
206:     visible += countVisibleDescendants(child)
207:   }
208:   return visible
209: }
210: 
211: export function getProgressSnapshot(
212:   node: TreeNode,
213: ): { done: number; total: number; percent: number; visible: number } | null {
214:   const descendants = collectDescendants(node)
215:   if (descendants.length === 0) {
216:     return null
217:   }
218: 
219:   const done = descendants.filter((child) => isDoneStatus(child.status)).length
220:   const total = descendants.length
221:   const percent = Math.round((done / total) * 100)
222:   const visible = countVisibleDescendants(node)
223: 
224:   return { done, total, percent, visible }
225: }
226: 
227: export function getRelatedIssueLabel(
228:   issueId: string,
229:   treeNodeIndex: Map<string, TreeNode>,
230: ): string {
231:   const relatedNode = treeNodeIndex.get(issueId)
232:   if (!relatedNode) {
233:     return issueId
234:   }
235:   return `${issueId} (${formatStatusLabel(relatedNode.status)})`
236: }
237: 
238: export function formatRelatedIssueList(
239:   issueIds: string[],
240:   treeNodeIndex: Map<string, TreeNode>,
241:   maxItems = 4,
242: ): string {
243:   if (issueIds.length === 0) {
244:     return 'none'
245:   }
246: 
247:   const shown = issueIds
248:     .slice(0, maxItems)
249:     .map((issueId) => getRelatedIssueLabel(issueId, treeNodeIndex))
250:   const remaining = issueIds.length - shown.length
251:   if (remaining > 0) {
252:     return `${shown.join(', ')} +${remaining} more`
253:   }
254:   return shown.join(', ')
255: }
256: 
257: export function getDependencyExplanation(
258:   node: TreeNode,
259:   treeNodeIndex: Map<string, TreeNode>,
260: ): { headline: string; detail: string; tone: string } {
261:   if (node.blocked_by.length > 0) {
262:     const blockers = formatRelatedIssueList(node.blocked_by, treeNodeIndex)
263:     return {
264:       headline: `Blocked by ${node.blocked_by.length} upstream issue${node.blocked_by.length === 1 ? '' : 's'}.`,
265:       detail: `This work cannot complete until these blockers resolve: ${blockers}`,
266:       tone: 'text-amber-500',
267:     }
268:   }
269: 
270:   if (node.blocks.length > 0) {
271:     const downstream = formatRelatedIssueList(node.blocks, treeNodeIndex)
272:     return {
273:       headline: `Ready and unblocks ${node.blocks.length} downstream issue${node.blocks.length === 1 ? '' : 's'}.`,
274:       detail: `Completing this item unlocks: ${downstream}`,
275:       tone: 'text-blue-500',
276:     }
277:   }
278: 
279:   return {
280:     headline: 'No direct blocking dependencies.',
281:     detail: 'This issue can be worked independently within the current graph scope.',
282:     tone: 'text-emerald-500',
283:   }
284: }
285: 
286: export function shortenModel(model?: string): string {
287:   if (!model) return 'model unknown'
288:   return model.split('/').pop()?.split('-').slice(0, 3).join('-') || model
289: }
290: 
291: function formatIssueAge(createdAt?: string): string | null {
292:   if (!createdAt) return null
293:   const created = new Date(createdAt)
294:   if (Number.isNaN(created.getTime())) return null
295: 
296:   const ageMs = Date.now() - created.getTime()
297:   if (ageMs < 60_000) return 'opened just now'
298: 
299:   const minutes = Math.floor(ageMs / 60_000)
300:   if (minutes < 60) return `opened ${minutes}m ago`
301: 
302:   const hours = Math.floor(minutes / 60)
303:   if (hours < 24) return `opened ${hours}h ago`
304: 
305:   const days = Math.floor(hours / 24)
306:   return `opened ${days}d ago`
307: }
308: 
309: export function getInProgressSubline(
310:   node: TreeNode,
311:   runningAgentDetailsByIssueId: Map<string, RunningAgentDetails>,
312: ): { text: string; tone: string } | null {
313:   if (node.status.toLowerCase() !== 'in_progress') {
314:     return null
315:   }
316: 
317:   const activeAgent = runningAgentDetailsByIssueId.get(node.id)
318:   const phase = node.active_agent?.phase || activeAgent?.phase
319:   const runtime = node.active_agent?.runtime || activeAgent?.runtime
320:   const model = node.active_agent?.model || activeAgent?.model
321: 
322:   if (phase || runtime || model) {
323:     const phaseText = phase || 'active'
324:     const runtimeText = runtime || 'runtime unknown'
325:     const modelText = shortenModel(model)
326:     return {
327:       text: `${phaseText} · ${runtimeText} · ${modelText}`,
328:       tone: 'text-blue-500/90',
329:     }
330:   }
331: 
332:   // Guard against null/undefined badges before checking specific types
333:   if (node.attentionBadge && (node.attentionBadge === 'verify' || node.attentionBadge === 'likely_done')) {
334:     return {
335:       text: 'Awaiting review (Phase: Complete)',
336:       tone: 'text-emerald-500/90',
337:     }
338:   }
339: 
340:   return {
341:     text: 'unassigned',
342:     tone: 'text-amber-500/90',
343:   }
344: }
345: 
346: export function getPriorityVariant(
347:   priority: number,
348: ): 'destructive' | 'secondary' | 'outline' {
349:   if (priority === 0) return 'destructive'
350:   if (priority === 1) return 'secondary'
351:   return 'outline'
352: }
353: 
354: export function getTypeBadge(type: string): string {
355:   switch (type.toLowerCase()) {
356:     case 'epic':
357:       return 'bg-purple-500/10 text-purple-500'
358:     case 'feature':
359:       return 'bg-blue-500/10 text-blue-500'
360:     case 'bug':
361:       return 'bg-red-500/10 text-red-500'
362:     case 'task':
363:       return 'bg-green-500/10 text-green-500'
364:     case 'question':
365:       return 'bg-yellow-500/10 text-yellow-500'
366:     default:
367:       return 'bg-muted text-muted-foreground'
368:   }
369: }
370: 
371: export function getAgentStatusIcon(agent: any): { icon: string; color: string } {
372:   const health = computeAgentHealth(agent)
373: 
374:   if (health.status === 'critical') {
375:     return { icon: '🚨', color: 'text-red-500' }
376:   }
377:   if (health.status === 'warning') {
378:     return { icon: '⚠️', color: 'text-yellow-500' }
379:   }
380: 
381:   if (agent.is_processing) {
382:     return { icon: '◉', color: 'text-blue-500 animate-pulse' }
383:   }
384:   if (agent.status === 'idle') {
385:     return { icon: '⏸', color: 'text-muted-foreground' }
386:   }
387:   return { icon: '▶', color: 'text-blue-500' }
388: }

(End of file - total 388 lines)
</content>

part.text:
**Planning Playwright test with API stub**

I'm planning to add a Playwright test that intercepts the API call to /api/beads/graph to stub its response, enabling verification of dependency chain ordering and gate separator placement on the work-graph page. This approach ensures tests run without requiring the actual backend.

**Confirming API stubbing approach**

The API calls use a different origin, so stubbing must target absolute URLs like `https://localhost:3348/api/beads/graph`. Other related endpoints also need stubbing to prevent network errors, so I'll review existing tests to adopt their stubbing patterns before implementing.

part.text:
**Planning stubbed Playwright test**

Tests rely on a dev backend at port 5188, so to avoid failures, I'll add a minimal Playwright test that stubs relevant API routes under `/work-graph` using a local baseURL, enabling the test to run without the full backend.

**Deciding to add Playwright test with comprehensive API stubs**

Unit testing is impractical due to missing harness, so I'll create a Playwright test that stubs all key API endpoints used on the `/work-graph` page by inspecting multiple store files to gather endpoint details and response shapes.

11: 	included_projects?: string[];
12: 	error?: string;
13: }
14: 
15: // Time filter options
16: export type TimeFilter = '12h' | '24h' | '48h' | '7d' | 'all';
17: 
18: // Filter state
19: export interface FilterState {
20: 	// Time-based filtering
21: 	since: TimeFilter;
22: 	// Project-based filtering (from orchestrator context)
23: 	project?: string;
24: 	includedProjects?: string[];
25: 	// Whether to auto-follow the orchestrator's context
26: 	followOrchestrator: boolean;
27: }
28: 
29: // Default filter state
30: const defaultFilterState: FilterState = {
31: 	since: '12h',
32: 	followOrchestrator: true,
33: };
34: 
35: // Load filter state from localStorage
36: function loadFilterState(): FilterState {
37: 	if (typeof window === 'undefined') return defaultFilterState;
38: 	try {
39: 		const stored = localStorage.getItem('orch-dashboard-filters');
40: 		if (stored) {
41: 			return { ...defaultFilterState, ...JSON.parse(stored) };
42: 		}
43: 	} catch (e) {
44: 		console.warn('Failed to load filter state:', e);
45: 	}
46: 	return defaultFilterState;
47: }
48: 
49: // Save filter state to localStorage
50: function saveFilterState(state: FilterState) {
51: 	if (typeof window === 'undefined') return;
52: 	try {
53: 		localStorage.setItem('orch-dashboard-filters', JSON.stringify(state));
54: 	} catch (e) {
55: 		console.warn('Failed to save filter state:', e);
56: 	}
57: }
58: 
59: // Context store
60: function createContextStore() {
61: 	const { subscribe, set } = writable<OrchestratorContext>({});
62: 
63: 	let pollInterval: ReturnType<typeof setInterval> | null = null;
64: 
65: 	return {
66: 		subscribe,
67: 		set,
68: 
69: 		// Fetch current context from API
70: 		async fetch(): Promise<void> {
71: 			try {
72: 				const response = await fetch(`${API_BASE}/api/context`);
73: 				if (!response.ok) {
74: 					throw new Error(`HTTP ${response.status}`);
75: 				}
76: 				const data = await response.json();
77: 				set(data);
78: 			} catch (error) {
79: 				console.error('Failed to fetch context:', error);
80: 				set({ error: String(error) });
81: 			}
82: 		},
83: 
84: 		// Start polling for context changes (500ms default)
85: 		startPolling(intervalMs: number = 500): void {
86: 			if (pollInterval) return;
87: 			
88: 			// Initial fetch
89: 			this.fetch();
90: 			
91: 			// Poll at interval
92: 			pollInterval = setInterval(() => {
93: 				this.fetch();
94: 			}, intervalMs);
95: 		},
96: 
97: 		// Stop polling
98: 		stopPolling(): void {
99: 			if (pollInterval) {
100: 				clearInterval(pollInterval);
101: 				pollInterval = null;
102: 			}
103: 		},
104: 	};
105: }
106: 
107: // Filter state store
108: function createFilterStore() {
109: 	const initial = loadFilterState();
110: 	const { subscribe, set, update } = writable<FilterState>(initial);
111: 
112: 	return {
113: 		subscribe,
114: 
115: 		// Set time filter
116: 		setTimeFilter(since: TimeFilter): void {
117: 			update((state) => {
118: 				const newState = { ...state, since };
119: 				saveFilterState(newState);
120: 				return newState;
121: 			});
122: 		},
123: 
124: 		// Set project filter (from orchestrator context)
125: 		setProjectFilter(project?: string, includedProjects?: string[]): void {
126: 			update((state) => {
127: 				const newState = { ...state, project, includedProjects };
128: 				saveFilterState(newState);
129: 				return newState;
130: 			});
131: 		},
132: 
133: 		// Toggle follow orchestrator mode
134: 		setFollowOrchestrator(follow: boolean): void {
135: 			update((state) => {
136: 				const newState = { ...state, followOrchestrator: follow };
137: 				saveFilterState(newState);
138: 				return newState;
139: 			});
140: 		},
141: 
142: 		// Clear project filter
143: 		clearProjectFilter(): void {
144: 			update((state) => {
145: 				const newState = { ...state, project: undefined, includedProjects: undefined };
146: 				saveFilterState(newState);
147: 				return newState;
148: 			});
149: 		},
150: 
151: 		// Reset to defaults
152: 		reset(): void {
153: 			set(defaultFilterState);
154: 			saveFilterState(defaultFilterState);
155: 		},
156: 	};
157: }
158: 
159: export const orchestratorContext = createContextStore();
160: export const filters = createFilterStore();
161: 
162: // Build API query string from filter state
163: export function buildFilterQueryString(state: FilterState): string {
164: 	const params = new URLSearchParams();
165: 
166: 	if (state.since && state.since !== 'all') {
167: 		params.set('since', state.since);
168: 	}
169: 
170: 	// Multi-project support: serialize includedProjects as comma-separated values
171: 	// This enables "orch-go special case" where orchestrator coordinates across 6 repos
172: 	if (state.includedProjects && state.includedProjects.length > 0) {
173: 		params.set('project', state.includedProjects.join(','));
174: 	} else if (state.project) {
175: 		// Fallback to single project if no includedProjects
176: 		params.set('project', state.project);
177: 	}
178: 
179: 	const query = params.toString();
180: 	return query ? `?${query}` : '';
181: }

84: export function computeDisplayState(agent: Agent): DisplayState {

192: // Agent store
193: function createAgentStore() {

211: 		// Fetch agents from orch-go API with in-flight tracking
212: 		// Only one fetch runs at a time. If called while fetching, queues a re-fetch
213: 		// after the current one completes (prevents request storm from SSE events).
214: 		// Optional queryString parameter for time/project filtering (e.g., "?since=12h&project=orch-go")
215: 		async fetch(queryString: string = ''): Promise<void> {
216: 			// If already fetching, mark that we need another fetch after this one
217: 			if (isFetching) {
218: 				needsRefetch = true;
219: 				return;
220: 			}
221: 			
222: 			isFetching = true;
223: 			needsRefetch = false;
224: 			currentFetchController = new AbortController();
225: 			
226: 			try {
227: 				const response = await fetch(`${API_BASE}/api/agents${queryString}`, {
228: 					signal: currentFetchController.signal
229: 				});

231: 				cacheValidation.updateFromResponse(response);
232: 				if (!response.ok) {

237: 				// but frontend expects object {type, text, timestamp}
238: 				const transformed = (data || []).map((agent: Agent & { current_activity?: string | Agent['current_activity'], last_activity_at?: string }) => {

240: 						return {

242: 							current_activity: {
243: 								type: 'text' as const,

245: 								timestamp: agent.last_activity_at ? new Date(agent.last_activity_at).getTime() : Date.now()

249: 					return agent;
250: 				});

254: 				if (error instanceof Error && error.name === 'AbortError') {

263: 				// If events arrived while we were fetching, do another fetch

265: 				if (needsRefetch) {

270: 		},
271: 		// Debounced fetch - prevents multiple rapid fetches from SSE events
272: 		fetchDebounced(): void {
273: 			if (fetchDebounceTimer) {

282: 		},
283: 		// Cancel pending operations - call on disconnect
284: 		cancelPending(): void {

294: 			isFetching = false;

381: // SSE event stream with deduplication
382: // Events with the same part.id are updated in place rather than added as duplicates
383: function createSSEStore() {
384: 	const { subscribe, update } = writable<SSEEvent[]>([]);
385: 
386: 	return {
387: 		subscribe,
388: 		// Add or update an event - data is the raw parsed SSE event from OpenCode
389: 		addOrUpdateEvent: (data: any) => {
390: 			// Extract stable ID from part.id if available (for message.part events)
391: 			const partId = extractEventId(data);
392: 			
393: 			update((events) => {
394: 				const eventWithId: SSEEvent = {
395: 					id: partId || generateSSEEventId(),
396: 					type: data.type || 'unknown',
397: 					properties: data.properties,
398: 					timestamp: Date.now()
399: 				};
400: 				
401: 				// If we have a stable part.id, try to update existing event
402: 				if (partId) {
403: 					const existingIndex = events.findIndex(e => e.id === partId);
404: 					if (existingIndex !== -1) {
405: 						// Update in place - this replaces the old version with new data
406: 						const newEvents = [...events];
407: 						newEvents[existingIndex] = eventWithId;
408: 						return newEvents;
409: 					}
410: 				}
411: 				
412: 				// New event - add to list
413: 				const newEvents = [...events, eventWithId];
414: 				// Keep last 1000 events (global across all agents)
415: 				return newEvents.slice(-1000);
416: 			});
417: 		},
418: 		// Legacy addEvent for backwards compatibility
419: 		addEvent: (event: Omit<SSEEvent, 'id'>) => {
420: 			update((events) => {
421: 				const newEvents = [...events, { ...event, id: generateSSEEventId() }];
422: 				// Keep last 1000 events (global across all agents)
423: 				return newEvents.slice(-1000);
424: 			});
425: 		},
426: 		clear: () => {
427: 			update(() => []);
428: 		}
429: 	};
430: }
431: 
432: export const sseEvents = createSSEStore();
433: 
434: // Per-session event history store for hybrid SSE + API architecture.
435: // Stores historical events fetched from API separately from real-time SSE events.
436: // This enables:
437: // 1. Complete session history on-demand (survives page refresh)
438: // 2. Per-agent history without global buffer dilution
439: // 3. Seamless merge with real-time SSE events
440: interface SessionHistory {
441: 	events: SSEEvent[];
442: 	loading: boolean;
443: 	loaded: boolean;
444: 	error: string | null;
445: }
446: 
447: function createSessionHistoryStore() {
448: 	const { subscribe, update } = writable<Map<string, SessionHistory>>(new Map());
449: 	
450: 	return {
451: 		subscribe,
452: 		// Fetch historical events for a session from the orch-go API
453: 		async fetchHistory(sessionId: string): Promise<SSEEvent[]> {
454: 			// Check if already loaded or loading
455: 			let currentState: Map<string, SessionHistory> = new Map();
456: 			update(state => {
457: 				currentState = state;
458: 				return state;
459: 			});
460: 			
461: 			const existing = currentState.get(sessionId);
462: 			if (existing?.loaded || existing?.loading) {
463: 				return existing.events;
464: 			}
465: 			
466: 			// Mark as loading
467: 			update(state => {
468: 				const newState = new Map(state);
469: 				newState.set(sessionId, { events: [], loading: true, loaded: false, error: null });
470: 				return newState;
471: 			});
472: 			
473: 			try {
474: 				const response = await fetch(`${API_BASE}/api/session/${sessionId}/messages`);
475: 				if (!response.ok) {
476: 					throw new Error(`HTTP ${response.status}: ${response.statusText}`);
477: 				}
478: 				const events: SSEEvent[] = await response.json();
479: 				
480: 				// Store the fetched events
481: 				update(state => {
482: 					const newState = new Map(state);
483: 					newState.set(sessionId, { events, loading: false, loaded: true, error: null });
484: 					return newState;
485: 				});
486: 				
487: 				return events;
488: 			} catch (error) {
489: 				const errorMsg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Unknown error';
490: 				update(state => {
491: 					const newState = new Map(state);
492: 					newState.set(sessionId, { events: [], loading: false, loaded: false, error: errorMsg });
493: 					return newState;
494: 				});
495: 				console.error('Failed to fetch session history:', error);
496: 				return [];
497: 			}
498: 		},
499: 		// Get current state for a session
500: 		getState(sessionId: string): SessionHistory | undefined {
501: 			let state: SessionHistory | undefined;
502: 			update(current => {
503: 				state = current.get(sessionId);
504: 				return current;
505: 			});
506: 			return state;
507: 		},
508: 		// Clear history for a session (useful when session is deleted)
509: 		clearSession(sessionId: string): void {
510: 			update(state => {
511: 				const newState = new Map(state);
512: 				newState.delete(sessionId);
513: 				return newState;
514: 			});
515: 		},
516: 		// Clear all history
517: 		clear(): void {
518: 			update(() => new Map());
519: 		}
520: 	};
521: }
522: 
523: export const sessionHistory = createSessionHistoryStore();
524: 
525: // Connection status
526: export const connectionStatus = writable<'connected' | 'disconnected' | 'connecting'>('disconnected');
527: 
528: // Selected agent for detail panel
529: export const selectedAgentId = writable<string | null>(null);
530: 
531: // Derived store for the selected agent
532: export const selectedAgent = derived([agents, selectedAgentId], ([$agents, $selectedAgentId]) => {
533: 	if (!$selectedAgentId) return null;
534: 	return $agents.find((a) => a.id === $selectedAgentId) || null;
535: });
536: 
537: // SSE connection manager - uses shared service for connection lifecycle
538: let sseConnection: SSEConnection | null = null;
539: 
540: // Build event listeners for the SSE connection
541: function buildSSEEventListeners(): Record<string, (event: MessageEvent) => void> {
542: 	const listeners: Record<string, (event: MessageEvent) => void> = {};
543: 
544: 	// Handle specific event types if sent with event: prefix
545: 	const eventTypes = ['session.status', 'session.created', 'session.deleted', 'agent.completed', 'agent.abandoned'];
546: 	eventTypes.forEach((type) => {
547: 		listeners[type] = (event: MessageEvent) => {
548: 			try {
549: 				const data = JSON.parse(event.data);
550: 				handleSSEEvent({ ...data, type });
551: 			} catch (e) {
552: 				console.error(`Failed to parse ${type} event:`, e);
553: 			}
554: 		};
555: 	});
556: 
557: 	// Handle custom events from our proxy
558: 	listeners['connected'] = (event: MessageEvent) => {
559: 		try {
560: 			const data = JSON.parse(event.data);
561: 			sseEvents.addEvent({
562: 				type: 'proxy.connected',
563: 				timestamp: Date.now()
564: 			});
565: 			console.log('SSE proxy connected to:', data.source);
566: 		} catch (e) {
567: 			console.log('SSE connected');
568: 		}
569: 	};
570: 
571: 	listeners['disconnected'] = () => {
572: 		connectionStatus.set('disconnected');
573: 		sseEvents.addEvent({
574: 			type: 'proxy.disconnected',
575: 			timestamp: Date.now()
576: 		});
577: 	};
578: 
579: 	listeners['error'] = (event: MessageEvent) => {
580: 		try {
581: 			const data = JSON.parse(event.data);
582: 			console.error('SSE proxy error:', data.error);
583: 			sseEvents.addEvent({
584: 				type: 'proxy.error',
585: 				timestamp: Date.now()
586: 			});
587: 		} catch (e) {
588: 			// Ignore parse errors for error events
589: 		}
590: 	};
591: 
592: 	return listeners;
593: }
594: 
595: // Callback to get current filter query string for fetches
596: // Set by the page component to provide dynamic filter state
597: let getFilterQueryString: (() => string) | null = null;
598: 
599: export function setFilterQueryStringCallback(callback: () => string): void {
600: 	getFilterQueryString = callback;
601: }
602: 
603: export function connectSSE(): void {
604: 	// Create connection if not exists
605: 	if (!sseConnection) {
606: 		sseConnection = createSSEConnection(`${API_BASE}/api/events`, {
607: 			onOpen: () => {
608: 				connectionStatus.set('connected');
609: 				// Fetch agents on connection to get current state (with filters if available)
610: 				const queryString = getFilterQueryString ? getFilterQueryString() : '';
611: 				agents.fetch(queryString).catch(console.error);
612: 			},
613: 			onMessage: (event: MessageEvent) => {
614: 				try {
615: 					const data = JSON.parse(event.data);
616: 					handleSSEEvent(data);
617: 				} catch (e) {
618: 					// Non-JSON data, create simple event
619: 					sseEvents.addEvent({
620: 						type: 'raw',
621: 						timestamp: Date.now()
622: 					});
623: 				}
624: 			},
625: 			onDisconnect: () => {
626: 				connectionStatus.set('disconnected');
627: 			},
628: 			eventListeners: buildSSEEventListeners(),
629: 			reconnectDelayMs: 5000,
630: 			autoReconnect: true
631: 		});
632: 
633: 		// Sync the connection status from the service to our local store
634: 		sseConnection.status.subscribe((status) => {
635: 			connectionStatus.set(status);
636: 		});
637: 	}
638: 
639: 	// Mark as connecting and initiate connection
640: 	connectionStatus.set('connecting');
641: 	sseConnection.connect();
642: }
643: 
644: function handleSSEEvent(data: any) {
645: 	// Use addOrUpdateEvent for proper deduplication of streaming events
646: 	// Events with the same part.id will update in place rather than duplicate
647: 	sseEvents.addOrUpdateEvent(data);
648: 
649: 	// Handle message.part and message.part.updated events - update agent activity in real-time
650: 	// message.part: text streaming (incremental text chunks)
651: 	// message.part.updated: tool state changes (running -> completed)
652: 	if ((data.type === 'message.part' || data.type === 'message.part.updated') && data.properties) {
653: 		const part = data.properties.part;
654: 		// sessionID is nested inside the part object
655: 		const sessionID = part?.sessionID;
656: 		
657: 		if (sessionID && part && part.type) {
658: 			// Update agent activity and set is_processing=true (agent is actively responding)
659: 			// Only update active agents to prevent stale pulsing on completed agents
660: 			// (late SSE events can arrive after agent status changes to completed)
661: 			agents.update((agentList) => {
662: 				return agentList.map((agent) => {
663: 					// Match by session_id AND only update active agents
664: 					if (agent.session_id === sessionID && agent.status === 'active') {
665: 						return {
666: 							...agent,
667: 							is_processing: true, // Agent is actively generating response
668: 							current_activity: {
669: 								type: part.type,
670: 								text: part.text || extractActivityText(part),
671: 								timestamp: Date.now()
672: 							}
673: 						};
674: 					}
675: 					return agent;
676: 				});
677: 			});
678: 		}
679: 	}
680: 
681: 	// Handle session.status events - update is_processing based on busy/idle state
682: 	// Uses debounced clear to prevent rapid flapping between busy/idle states
683: 	if (data.type === 'session.status' && data.properties) {
684: 		const sessionID = data.properties.sessionID;
685: 		const statusType = data.properties.status?.type;
686: 		
687: 		if (sessionID && statusType) {
688: 			const isProcessing = statusType === 'busy';
689: 			
690: 			if (isProcessing) {
691: 				// Setting to busy: immediate, and cancel any pending clear timer
692: 				const pendingClear = processingClearTimers.get(sessionID);
693: 				if (pendingClear) {
694: 					clearTimeout(pendingClear);
695: 					processingClearTimers.delete(sessionID);
696: 				}
697: 				
698: 				agents.update((agentList) => {
699: 					return agentList.map((agent) => {
700: 						if (agent.session_id === sessionID && agent.status === 'active') {
701: 							return { ...agent, is_processing: true };
702: 						}
703: 						return agent;
704: 					});
705: 				});
706: 			} else {
707: 				// Setting to idle: debounced to prevent rapid flapping
708: 				// Cancel any existing timer first
709: 				const existingTimer = processingClearTimers.get(sessionID);
710: 				if (existingTimer) {
711: 					clearTimeout(existingTimer);
712: 				}
713: 				
714: 				// Set new timer for delayed clear
715: 				const timer = setTimeout(() => {
716: 					processingClearTimers.delete(sessionID);
717: 					agents.update((agentList) => {
718: 						return agentList.map((agent) => {
719: 							if (agent.session_id === sessionID) {
720: 								return {
721: 									...agent,
722: 									is_processing: false
723: 									// Note: Don't clear current_activity here - the last activity
724: 									// should persist so users can see what the agent was doing.
725: 									// Clearing it causes "Starting up..." to show for completed agents.
726: 								};
727: 							}
728: 							return agent;
729: 						});
730: 					});
731: 				}, PROCESSING_CLEAR_DELAY_MS);
732: 				
733: 				processingClearTimers.set(sessionID, timer);
734: 			}
735: 		}
736: 	}
737: 
738: 	// Handle lifecycle events - refresh agent list (debounced to prevent race conditions)
739: 	// Note: session.status is NOT included because it's already handled via local state
740: 	// updates above (lines 510-562). Including it would cause redundant fetches since
741: 	// session.status fires on every busy/idle toggle (high frequency).
742: 	const refreshEvents = [
743: 		'session.created',
744: 		'session.deleted',
745: 		'agent.completed',
746: 		'agent.abandoned'
747: 	];
748: 	if (refreshEvents.includes(data.type)) {
749: 		agents.fetchDebounced();
750: 	}
751: }
752: 
753: // Extract displayable text from message part
754: function extractActivityText(part: any): string {
755: 	// For tool invocations, show tool name and function
756: 	if (part.type === 'tool-invocation' || part.type === 'tool') {
757: 		if (part.tool) {
758: 			return `Using ${part.tool}${part.function ? `.${part.function}` : ''}`;
759: 		}
760: 		return 'Using tool';
761: 	}
762: 	
763: 	// For step-start/finish, show step info
764: 	if (part.type === 'step-start') {
765: 		return 'Starting step...';
766: 	}
767: 	if (part.type === 'step-finish') {
768: 		return 'Completed step';
769: 	}
770: 	
771: 	// For reasoning, show preview
772: 	if (part.type === 'reasoning' && part.text) {
773: 		return part.text.substring(0, 100);
774: 	}
775: 	
776: 	// Default: show part type
777: 	return part.type.replace(/-/g, ' ');
778: }
779: 
780: // Create a new beads issue (for follow-ups from synthesis recommendations)
781: export async function createIssue(title: string, description?: string, labels?: string[]): Promise<{ id: string; title: string } | null> {
782: 	try {
783: 		const response = await fetch(`${API_BASE}/api/issues`, {
784: 			method: 'POST',
785: 			headers: {
786: 				'Content-Type': 'application/json',
787: 			},
788: 			body: JSON.stringify({
789: 				title,
790: 				description,
791: 				labels: labels || ['triage:ready'],
792: 				issue_type: 'task',
793: 			}),
794: 		});
795: 		
796: 		if (!response.ok) {
797: 			const errorData = await response.json();
798: 			throw new Error(errorData.error || `HTTP ${response.status}`);
799: 		}
800: 		
801: 		const data = await response.json();
802: 		if (data.success) {
803: 			return { id: data.id, title: data.title };
804: 		}
805: 		throw new Error(data.error || 'Unknown error');
806: 	} catch (error) {
807: 		console.error('Failed to create issue:', error);
808: 		throw error;
809: 	}
810: }
811: 
812: export function disconnectSSE(): void {
813: 	if (sseConnection) {
814: 		sseConnection.disconnect();
815: 	}
816: 	// Cancel any pending fetch operations
817: 	agents.cancelPending();
818: 	// Clear all pending processing clear timers to prevent memory leaks
819: 	processingClearTimers.forEach((timer) => clearTimeout(timer));
820: 	processingClearTimers.clear();
821: 	connectionStatus.set('disconnected');
822: }

<content>1: import { writable } from 'svelte/store';

45: }
46: 
47: function createAttentionStore() {
48: 	const { subscribe, set, update } = writable<AttentionStoreData>({
49: 		completedIssues: [],
50: 		signals: new Map()
51: 	});
52: 
53: 	return {
54: 		subscribe,
55: 		
56: 		async fetch(projectDir?: string): Promise<void> {
57: 			try {
58: 				const url = new URL(`${API_BASE}/api/attention`);
59: 				if (projectDir) {
60: 					url.searchParams.set('project', projectDir);
61: 				}
62: 
63: 				const response = await fetch(url.toString());
64: 				if (!response.ok) {
65: 					console.error('Failed to fetch attention signals:', response.statusText);
66: 					return;
67: 				}
68: 
69: 				const data: AttentionAPIResponse = await response.json();
70: 				
71: 				// Transform API response into store data
72: 				const completedIssues: CompletedIssue[] = [];
73: 				const signals = new Map<string, AttentionSignal>();
74: 
75: 			for (const item of data.items) {
76: 				// Map signal type to badge type
77: 				const badge = mapSignalToBadge(item);
78: 				
79: 				// Only add to signals map if badge is not null
80: 				// (filters out informational signals like 'issue-ready' that don't need visual badges)
81: 				if (badge !== null) {
82: 					signals.set(item.subject, {
83: 						badge,
84: 						reason: item.summary
85: 					});
86: 				}
87: 
88: 				// Add to completedIssues if recently-closed
89: 				if (item.signal === 'recently-closed') {
90: 					completedIssues.push({
91: 						id: item.subject,
92: 						title: item.summary,
93: 						completed_at: item.collected_at,
94: 						project: projectDir || ''
95: 					});
96: 				}
97: 			}
98: 
99: 				set({ completedIssues, signals });
100: 			} catch (err) {

102: 			}
103: 		},
104: 		
105: 		set,
106: 		update
107: 	};

110: /**
111:  * Map attention signal types to badge types based on signal and metadata
112:  */
113: function mapSignalToBadge(item: AttentionItemResponse): AttentionBadgeType | null {
114: 	// Handle recently-closed with verification status
115: 	if (item.signal === 'recently-closed') {
116: 		const verificationStatus = item.metadata?.verification_status;
117: 		if (verificationStatus === 'verified') {
118: 			return 'recently_closed';
119: 		}
120: 		if (verificationStatus === 'needs_fix') {
121: 			return 'verify_failed';
122: 		}
123: 		// Default to 'verify' for unverified or no status
124: 		return 'verify';
125: 	}
126: 
127: 	// Direct signal to badge mappings
128: 	switch (item.signal) {
129: 		case 'likely-done':
130: 			return 'likely_done';
131: 		case 'stuck':
132: 			return 'stuck';
133: 		case 'unblocked':
134: 			return 'unblocked';
135: 		case 'verify':
136: 			return 'verify';
137: 		case 'verify-failed':
138: 			return 'verify_failed';
139: 		default:
140: 			// Return null for unmapped signals (e.g., 'issue-ready', 'stale', etc.)
141: 			// These are informational signals that don't need visual badges
142: 			return null;
143: 	}
144: }

148: export function formatRelativeTime(timestamp: string): string {
149: 	const now = new Date();
150: 	const then = new Date(timestamp);
151: 	const diffMs = now.getTime() - then.getTime();
152: 	const diffMins = Math.floor(diffMs / 60000);
153: 	
154: 	if (diffMins < 1) return 'just now';
155: 	if (diffMins < 60) return `${diffMins}m ago`;
156: 	
157: 	const diffHours = Math.floor(diffMins / 60);
158: 	if (diffHours < 24) return `${diffHours}h ago`;
159: 	
160: 	const diffDays = Math.floor(diffHours / 24);
161: 	return `${diffDays}d ago`;
162: }

<content>1: import { writable } from 'svelte/store';
2: 
3: // API configuration - HTTPS for HTTP/2 multiplexing
4: const API_BASE = 'https://localhost:3348';
5: 
6: // Verification status for daemon gate visibility
7: export interface DaemonVerificationStatus {
8: 	is_paused: boolean; // Whether daemon is paused due to verification threshold
9: 	completions_since_verification: number; // Count of auto-completions since last verification
10: 	threshold: number; // Maximum auto-completions before pausing
11: 	remaining_before_pause: number; // Completions allowed before pause
12: 	last_verification?: string; // ISO 8601 timestamp of last verification
13: 	last_verification_ago?: string; // Human-readable time since last verification
14: }
15: 
16: // Daemon status response from /api/daemon
17: export interface DaemonStatus {
18: 	running: boolean;
19: 	status?: string; // "running", "stalled", or undefined if not running
20: 	last_poll?: string; // ISO 8601 timestamp
21: 	last_poll_ago?: string; // Human-readable time since last poll
22: 	last_spawn?: string; // ISO 8601 timestamp
23: 	last_spawn_ago?: string; // Human-readable time since last spawn
24: 	ready_count: number; // Issues ready to process
25: 	capacity_max: number; // Maximum concurrent agents
26: 	capacity_used: number; // Currently active agents
27: 	capacity_free: number; // Available slots
28: 	issues_per_hour?: number; // Processing rate (future)
29: 	verification?: DaemonVerificationStatus; // Verification gate status
30: }
31: 
32: // Daemon store
33: function createDaemonStore() {
34: 	const { subscribe, set } = writable<DaemonStatus | null>(null);
35: 
36: 	return {
37: 		subscribe,
38: 		set,
39: 		// Fetch daemon status from orch-go API
40: 		async fetch(): Promise<void> {
41: 			try {
42: 				const response = await fetch(`${API_BASE}/api/daemon`);
43: 				if (!response.ok) {
44: 					throw new Error(`HTTP ${response.status}: ${response.statusText}`);
45: 				}
46: 				const data = await response.json();
47: 				set(data);
48: 			} catch (error) {
49: 				console.error('Failed to fetch daemon status:', error);
50: 				// Set to not running on error
51: 				set({
52: 					running: false,
53: 					ready_count: 0,
54: 					capacity_max: 0,
55: 					capacity_used: 0,
56: 					capacity_free: 0
57: 				});
58: 			}
59: 		}
60: 	};
61: }
62: 
63: export const daemon = createDaemonStore();
64: 
65: // Helper to get status emoji
66: export function getDaemonEmoji(status: DaemonStatus | null): string {
67: 	if (!status?.running) return '💤'; // Not running
68: 	if (status.status === 'stalled') return '⚠️'; // Stalled
69: 	if (status.capacity_free === 0) return '🔴'; // At capacity
70: 	return '🟢'; // Running with capacity
71: }
72: 
73: // Helper to get status label
74: export function getDaemonLabel(status: DaemonStatus | null): string {
75: 	if (!status?.running) return 'stopped';
76: 	if (status.status === 'stalled') return 'stalled';
77: 	return 'running';
78: }
79: 
80: // Helper to get capacity display
81: export function getDaemonCapacity(status: DaemonStatus | null): string {
82: 	if (!status?.running) return '';
83: 	return `${status.capacity_used}/${status.capacity_max}`;
84: }

**Evaluating SSE handling in tests**

Considering intercepting the SSE endpoint to avoid actual connections during tests, possibly by stubbing it with a minimal event stream or failing it, accepting that the UI might show a disconnect banner but still allow loading stubbed data.

**Designing comprehensive dependency chain test**

I'm outlining a Playwright test that stubs all relevant APIs with minimal data representing nodes A, B, C, D and their blocking relationships, then verifies the UI renders the dependency chain in correct topological order with proper root markers and gate separator placement. Next, I'll create the test file incorporating these API mocks and DOM assertions.

**Analyzing inverted topological sorting cause**

The likely root issue is inconsistent interpretation of edge directions: edges define "from" as dependent on "to," but code treats "from blocks to," reversing dependency flow and causing inverted sorts; true fix requires correcting edge usage, but current instructions focus on reversing sort order and adjusting gate detection to align UI order with upstream-to-downstream flow.

**Confirming inversion cause and planning targeted fixes**

The root cause of inverted topological order is reversed edge interpretation causing downstream nodes to appear as roots; the minimal fix is to reverse sort order by layer in the dependency view’s node sorting and enhance gate detection to mark convergence nodes properly—next, implement these changes in sorting and gate logic as specified.

**Detailing gate detection and root marker adjustments**

Planning to implement gate detection by identifying convergence nodes with multiple blockers within the chain and marking only one as gate, replacing leaf-based detection; also updating the Svelte component to show root markers exclusively on upstream nodes without blockers and refining gate separator placement to appear only above gate items within dependency chains.

part.text:
**Analyzing test failure from changed dependency order**

The test's expected node order conflicts with the new DFS pre-order traversal that visits a child before a sibling; this causes downstream nodes like 4 to appear before upstream siblings like 3. The traversal now outputs 1,2,4,3 instead of expected 1,2,3,4, suggesting the algorithm needs adjustment to preserve upstream-first topological ordering in the view.

**Evaluating traversal order and prefix challenges**

The traversal using DFS produces connectors aligned with tree structure but places downstream convergence gates before some upstream siblings, violating the desired upstream-to-downstream reading order. Switching to breadth-first or global depth sorting could fix ordering but would break prefix connectors. A possible solution is adjusting traversal or node attachment to place convergence gates later while preserving prefix correctness, but the trade-offs between visual clarity and strict execution order remain complex.

10: // Node represents a minimal graph node for dependency computations.

24: // ComputeLayers assigns topological layers using longest-path layering.
25: // Layer 0 indicates nodes with no blocking dependencies.
26: // Only "blocks" dependencies are considered for layering (matching beads computeLayout()).
27: func ComputeLayers(nodes []Node, edges []Edge) map[string]int {
28: 	layers := make(map[string]int, len(nodes))
29: 	nodeSet := make(map[string]struct{}, len(nodes))
30: 	dependsOn := make(map[string][]string, len(nodes))
31: 
32: 	for _, node := range nodes {
33: 		layers[node.ID] = -1
34: 		nodeSet[node.ID] = struct{}{}
35: 	}
36: 
37: 	for _, edge := range edges {
38: 		if normalizeEdgeType(edge.Type) != DepBlocks {
39: 			continue
40: 		}
41: 		if _, ok := nodeSet[edge.From]; !ok {
42: 			continue
43: 		}
44: 		if _, ok := nodeSet[edge.To]; !ok {
45: 			continue
46: 		}
47: 		dependsOn[edge.From] = append(dependsOn[edge.From], edge.To)
48: 	}
49: 
50: 	changed := true
51: 	for changed {
52: 		changed = false
53: 		for _, node := range nodes {
54: 			if layers[node.ID] >= 0 {
55: 				continue
56: 			}
57: 
58: 			deps := dependsOn[node.ID]
59: 			if len(deps) == 0 {
60: 				layers[node.ID] = 0
61: 				changed = true
62: 				continue
63: 			}
64: 
65: 			maxDepLayer := -1
66: 			allAssigned := true
67: 			for _, depID := range deps {
68: 				depLayer, ok := layers[depID]
69: 				if !ok || depLayer < 0 {
70: 					allAssigned = false
71: 					break
72: 				}
73: 				if depLayer > maxDepLayer {
74: 					maxDepLayer = depLayer
75: 				}
76: 			}
77: 
78: 			if allAssigned {
79: 				layers[node.ID] = maxDepLayer + 1
80: 				changed = true
81: 			}
82: 		}
83: 	}
84: 
85: 	for _, node := range nodes {
86: 		if layers[node.ID] < 0 {
87: 			layers[node.ID] = 0
88: 		}
89: 	}
90: 
91: 	return layers
92: }
93: 
94: // ComputeEffectivePriority computes the minimum priority reachable downstream
95: // through blocking dependencies (transitive closure). Lower priority values
96: // indicate higher urgency (P0 < P1 < ...).
97: func ComputeEffectivePriority(nodes []Node, edges []Edge) map[string]int {
98: 	blockedBy := make(map[string][]string, len(nodes))
99: 	priorityByID := make(map[string]int, len(nodes))
100: 	for _, node := range nodes {
101: 		priorityByID[node.ID] = node.Priority
102: 	}
103: 
104: 	for _, edge := range edges {
105: 		if !affectsReadyWork(edge.Type) {
106: 			continue
107: 		}
108: 		if _, ok := priorityByID[edge.From]; !ok {
109: 			continue
110: 		}
111: 		if _, ok := priorityByID[edge.To]; !ok {
112: 			continue
113: 		}
114: 		blockedBy[edge.To] = append(blockedBy[edge.To], edge.From)
115: 	}
116: 
117: 	result := make(map[string]int, len(nodes))
118: 	visiting := make(map[string]bool, len(nodes))
119: 
120: 	var dfs func(string) int
121: 	dfs = func(id string) int {
122: 		if val, ok := result[id]; ok {
123: 			return val
124: 		}
125: 		if visiting[id] {
126: 			return priorityByID[id]
127: 		}
128: 		visiting[id] = true
129: 
130: 		minPriority := priorityByID[id]
131: 		for _, child := range blockedBy[id] {
132: 			childPriority := dfs(child)
133: 			if childPriority < minPriority {
134: 				minPriority = childPriority
135: 			}
136: 		}
137: 
138: 		visiting[id] = false
139: 		result[id] = minPriority
140: 		return minPriority
141: 	}
142: 
143: 	for _, node := range nodes {
144: 		result[node.ID] = dfs(node.ID)
145: 	}
146: 
147: 	return result
148: }
149: 
150: func normalizeEdgeType(edgeType string) string {
151: 	if edgeType == "" {
152: 		return DepBlocks
153: 	}
154: 	return edgeType
155: }
156: 
157: func affectsReadyWork(edgeType string) bool {
158: 	switch normalizeEdgeType(edgeType) {
159: 	case DepBlocks, DepParentChild, DepConditionalBlocks, DepWaitsFor:
160: 		return true
161: 	default:
162: 		return false
163: 	}
164: }

part.text:
**Identifying inconsistency in dependency direction**

buildTree uses a reversed edge direction for blocked_by compared to the dependency graph, causing inconsistency with buildDependencyView and potentially breaking gate detection relying on blocked_by; this suggests buildTree’s blocked_by assignment may need fixing to align with the correct dependency direction.

**Evaluating impact of fixing buildTree direction**

Fixing buildTree’s blocked_by direction would affect gate detection and other features relying on it, posing a broader change beyond current scope; an alternative is reworking gate detection to avoid blocked_by dependency or verifying the API edge direction before deciding on adjustments.

part.text:
**Reevaluating gate detection using dependency direction**

The current use of blocked_by is reversed and misrepresents dependencies, breaking gate detection that relies on blockers. Instead, gate presence should be inferred directly from the dependency graph's structure—specifically the leaf at max depth in the dependency tree excluding the root—since the build now uses correct orientation. This approach avoids relying on reversed blocked_by and aligns gate logic with actual dependency relationships.

**Refining gate detection to use max-depth leaf only**

Plan to remove the blocked_by dependency from gate detection and rely solely on identifying a unique leaf node at max depth with depth > 0, simplifying logic and aligning with orientation-based root markers. This includes dropping visibleNodeMap if unused to streamline the code while supporting gate path ordering via the filtered visible leaf list.
